name: sv_wallfriction
description: Additional friction when running into walls

name: sv_gameplayfix_multiplethinks
description: Enables multiple thinks per entity per frame so small nextthink times are accurate. QuakeWorld mods expect a value of 1, while NQ expects 0.

name: sv_gameplayfix_stepdown
description: Attempt to step down steps, instead of only up them. Affects non-predicted movetype_walk.

name: sv_gameplayfix_grenadebouncedownslopes
description: MOVETYPE_BOUNCE speeds are calculated relative to the impacted surface, instead of the vertical, reducing the chance of grenades just sitting there on slopes.

name: sv_gameplayfix_trappedwithin
description: Blocks further entity movement when an entity is already inside another entity. This ensures that bsp precision issues cannot allow the entity to completely pass through eg the world.

name: sv_gameplayfix_radialmaxvelocity
description: Applies maxvelocity radially instead of axially.

name: sv_gameplayfix_spawnbeforethinks
description: Fixes an issue where player thinks (including Pre+Post) can be called before PutClientInServer. Unfortunately at least one mod depends upon PreThink being called first in order to correctly determine spawn positions.

name: dpcompat_noretouchground
description: Prevents entities that are already standing on an entity from touching the same entity again.

name: pm_stepheight
description: If empty, the value

name: pm_bunnyspeedcap
description: 0 or 1, ish. If the player is traveling faster than this speed while turning, their velocity will be gracefully reduced to match their current maxspeed. You can still rocket-jump to gain high velocity, but turning will reduce your speed back to the max. This can be used to disable bunny hopping.

name: pm_watersinkspeed
description: This is the speed that players will sink at while inactive in water. Empty means 60.

name: pm_flyfriction
description: Amount of friction that applies in fly or 6dof mode. Empty means 4.

name: pm_slidefix
description: Fixes an issue when walking down slopes (ie: so they act more like slopes and not a series of steps)

name: pm_slidyslopes
description: Replicates NQ behaviour, where players will slowly slide down ramps. Generally requires 'pm_noround 1' too, otherwise the effect rounds to nothing.

name: pm_bunnyfriction
description: Replicates NQ behaviour, ensuring that there's at least a frame of friction while jumping - friction is proportional to tick rate.

name: pm_autobunny
description: Players will continue jumping without needing to release the jump button.

name: pm_airstep
description: Allows players to step up while jumping. This makes stairs more graceful but also increases potential jump heights.

name: pm_pground
description: Use persisten onground state instead of recalculating every frame.

name: pm_stepdown
description: Causes physics to stick to the ground, instead of constantly losing traction while going down steps.

name: pm_walljump
description: Allows the player to bounce off walls while arborne.

name: pm_edgefriction
description: Increases friction when about to walk over a cliff, so you're less likely to plummet by mistake. When empty defaults to 2, but uses a tracebox instead of a traceline to detect the drop.

name: sv_demoAutoRecord
description: If set, automatically record demos. -1: record on client connection only. 1+: record once there's this many active players on the server (or if connected to a remote server).

name: sv_demoUseCache
description: If set, demo data will be flushed only periodically

name: sv_demoMaxDirSize
description: Maximum allowed serverside storage space for mvds. set to blank to remove the limit. New demos cannot be recorded once this size is reached.

name: sv_demoMaxDirCount
description: Maximum allowed serverside mvds to record. Set to 0 to remove the limit. New demos cannot be recorded once this many demos have already been recorded.

name: sv_demoMaxDirAge
description: Maximum allowed age for demos, any older demos will be deleted when sv_demoClearOld is set (this doesn't prevent recording new demos).

name: sv_demoClearOld
description: Automatically delete demos to keep the demos count reasonable.

name: sv_demoPings
description: Interval between ping updates in mvds

name: sv_demoMaxSize
description: Demos will be truncated to be no larger than this size.

name: sv_demoExtensions
description: Enables protocol extensions within MVDs. This will cause older/non-fte clients to error upon playback. 0: off. 1: all extensions. 2: extensions also supported by a certain other engine.

name: sv_demoAutoCompress
description: Specifies whether to compress demos as they're recorded. 0 = no compression. 1 = gzip compression.

name: sv_demo_write_csqc
description: Writes a copy of the csprogs into recorded demos. This ensures that the demo can be played back despite future gamecode changes.

name: qtv_maxstreams
description: This is the maximum number of QTV clients/proxies that may be directly connected to the server. If empty then there is no limit. 0 disallows any streaming.

name: sys_colorconsole
description: Parse colour escapes, with ansi colours on stdout.

name: sys_timestamps
description: Show timesamps on stdout prints.

name: rank_autoadd
description: Automatically register players into the ranking system

name: rank_needlogin
description: If set to 1, prohibits players from joining if they're not yet registered. This will require an external mechanism to register users, presumably via rcon.

name: rank_filename
description: Specifies which file to use as a rankings database. Enables the ranking system if set.

name: rank_parms_first
description: Mod setting: first parm saved

name: rank_parms_last
description: Mod setting: the index of the last parm to be saved. Clamped to 32.

name: sv_autooffload
description: Automatically start the server in a separate process, so that sporadic or persistent gamecode slowdowns do not affect visual framerates (equivelent to the mapcluster command). Note: Offloaded servers have separate cvar+command states which may complicate usage.

name: sv_mintic
description: The minimum interval between running physics frames.

name: sv_maxtic
description: The maximum interval between running physics frames. If the value is too low, multiple physics interations might be run at a time (based upon sv_limittics). Set to 0 for fixed-interval ticks, which may be required if ODE is used.

name: sv_limittics
description: The maximum number of ticks that may be run within a frame, to allow the server to catch up if it stalled or if sv_maxtic is too low.

name: sv_nailhack
description: If set to 1, disables the nail entity networking optimisation. This hack was popularised by qizmo which recommends it for better compression. Also allows clients to interplate nail positions and add trails.

name: sv_nopvs
description: Set to 1 to ignore pvs on the server. This can make wallhacks more dangerous, so should only be used for debugging.

name: fraglog_public
description: Enables support for connectionless fraglog requests

name: fraglog_details
description: Bitmask 1: killer+killee names. 2: killer+killee teams 4:timestamp. 8:killer weapon 16:killer+killee guid. For compatibility, use 1(vanilla) or 7(mvdsv).

name: zombietime
description: Client slots will not be reused for this number of seconds.

name: sv_rconlim
description: Blocks repeated (invalid) rcon attempts.

name: sv_crypt_rcon
description: Controls whether the rcon password must be hashed or not. Hashed passwords also partially prevent replay attacks, but does NOT prevent malicious actors from reading the commands/results. 0: completely insecure. ONLY allows plain-text passwords. Do not use. 1: Mandatory hashing (recommended). Empty: Allow either, whether the password is secure or not is purely the client's responsibility/fault. Only use this for comptibility with old clients.

name: sv_timestamplen
description: Limits clock skew to reduce (delayed) replay attacks

name: allow_download
description: If 1, permits downloading. Set to 0 to unconditionally block *ALL* downloads from this server. You may wish to set sv_dlURL if you wish clients to still be able to download content.

name: allow_download_skins
description: 0 blocks downloading of any file in the skins/ directory

name: allow_download_models
description: 0 blocks downloading of any file in the progs/ or models/ directory

name: allow_download_sounds
description: 0 blocks downloading of any file in the sound/ directory

name: allow_download_particles
description: 0 blocks downloading of any file in the particles/ directory

name: allow_download_demos
description: 0 blocks downloading of any file in the demos/ directory

name: allow_download_maps
description: 0 blocks downloading of any file in the maps/ directory

name: allow_download_logs
description: 1 permits downloading files with the extension .log

name: allow_download_pakmaps
description: 0: clients may not download map files within the server's packages. 1: clients may download such files so long as the package is not deemed copyrighted, for compat with old clients that do not support package downloads. 2: client may download such files regardless of copyright state (WARNING! Should never be used!).

name: allow_download_pakcontents
description: 0: clients may not download non-map files within the server's packages. 1: clients may download such files so long as the package is not deemed copyrighted, for compat with old clients that do not support package downloads. 2: client may download such files regardless of copyright state (WARNING! ONLY for consistency with vanilla QuakeWorld!).

name: allow_download_root
description: If set, enables downloading from the root of the gamedir (not the basedir). This setting has a lower priority than extension-based checks.

name: allow_download_textures
description: 0 blocks downloading of any file in the textures/ directory

name: allow_download_packages
description: if 1, permits downloading files (from root directory or elsewhere) with known package extensions (eg: pak+pk3). Packages with a name starting 'pak' are covered by allow_download_copyrighted as well.

name: allow_download_refpackages
description: If set to 1, packages that contain files needed during spawn functions will be become 'referenced' and automatically downloaded to clients. This cvar should probably not be set if you have large packages that provide replacement pickup models on public servers. The path command will show a '(ref)' tag next to packages which clients will automatically attempt to download.

name: allow_download_wads
description: 0 blocks downloading of any file in the wads/ directory, or is in the root directory with the extension .wad

name: allow_download_configs
description: 1 allows downloading of config files, either with the extension .cfg or in the subdir configs/.

name: allow_download_locs
description: 0 blocks downloading of any file in the locs/ directory

name: allow_download_copyrighted
description: 0 blocks download of packages that are considered copyrighted. Specifically, this means packages with a leading 'pak' prefix on the filename. If you take your copyrights seriously, you should also set allow_download_pakmaps 0 and allow_download_pakcontents 0.

name: allow_download_other
description: 0 blocks downloading of any file that was not covered by any of the directory download blocks.

name: sv_public
description: -1: Fully blocks all inbound connections. 0: Disable subscribing to master servers (for private lan-only games). 1: Subscribe to public master servers. Your IP address will be listed publicly. Make sure your Router/NAT+Firewall are set to allow inbound connections.

name: sv_guidkey
description: If set, clients will calculate their GUID values against this string instead of the server's IP address. This allows consistency between multiple servers (for stats tracking), but do NOT treat the client's GUID as something that is secure.

name: sv_serverip
description: Set this cvar to the server's public ip address if the server is behind a firewall and cannot detect its own public address. Providing a port is required if the firewall/nat remaps it, but is otherwise optional.

name: sv_listen_qw
description: Specifies whether normal clients are allowed to connect.

name: sv_listen_nq
description: Allow new (net)quake clients to connect to the server. 0 = don't let them in. 1 = allow them in (WARNING: this allows 'qsmurf' DOS attacks). 2 = accept (net)quake clients by emulating a challenge (as secure as QW/Q2 but does not fully conform to the NQ protocol). You may also need to set net_enable_dtls if you wish for the rerelease's client to connect.

name: sv_listen_dp
description: Allows the server to respond with the DP-specific handshake protocol. Warning: this can potentially get confused with quake2, and results in race conditions with both vanilla netquake and quakeworld protocols. On the plus side, DP clients can usually be identified correctly, enabling a model+sound limit boost.

name: sv_reconnectlimit
description: Blocks dupe connection within the specified length of time .

name: sv_use_dns
description: Performs a reverse-dns lookup in order to report more info about where clients are connecting from.

name: sv_reportheartbeats
description: Print a notice each time a heartbeat is sent to a master server. When set to 2, the message will be displayed once.

name: sv_heartbeat_interval
description: Interval between heartbeats. Low values are abusive, high values may cause NAT/ghost issues.

name: sv_heartbeat_checks
description: Report when sv_public 1 fails due to PROBABLE router/NAT issues.

name: sv_minping
description: Simulate fake lag for any players with a ping under the value specified here. Value is in milliseconds.

name: sv_bigcoords
description: Uses floats for coordinates instead of 16bit values. Also boosts angle precision, so can be useful even on small maps. Affects clients thusly: QW: enforces a mandatory protocol extension DP: enables DPP7 protocol support NQ: uses RMQ protocol (protocol 999).

name: sv_calcphs
description: Enables culling of sound effects. 0=always skip phs. Sounds are globally broadcast. 1=always generate phs. Sounds are always culled. On large maps the phs will be dumped to disk. 2=On large single-player maps, generation of phs is skipped. Otherwise like option 1.

name: sv_showconnectionlessmessages
description: Display a line describing each connectionless message that arrives on the server. Primarily a debugging feature, but also potentially useful to admins.

name: sv_cullplayers_trace
description: Attempt to cull player entities using tracelines as an anti-wallhack.

name: sv_cullentities_trace
description: Attempt to cull non-player entities using tracelines as an extreeme anti-wallhack.

name: sv_phs
description: If 1, do not use the phs. It is generally better to use sv_calcphs instead, and leave this as 1.

name: sv_pupglow
description: Instructs clients to enable hexen2-style powerup pulsing.

name: sv_reliable_sound
description: Causes all sounds to be sent reliably, so they will not be missed due to packetloss. However, this will cause them to be delayed somewhat, and slightly bursty. This can be overriden using the 'rsnd' userinfo setting (either forced on or forced off). Note: this does not affect sounds attached to particle effects.

name: sv_csqcdebug
description: Inject packet size information for data directed to csqc.

name: sv_banproxies
description: If enabled, anyone connecting via known proxy software will be refused entry. This should aid with blocking aimbots, but is only reliable for certain public proxies.

name: sv_specprint
description: Bitfield that controls which player events spectators see when tracking that player. &1: spectators will see centerprints. &2: spectators will see sprints (pickup messages etc). &4: spectators will receive console commands, this is potentially risky. Individual spectators can use 'setinfo sp foo' to limit this setting.

name: sv_protocol
description: Specifies which protocol extensions to force. Clients which do not support the named protocols will be kicked. Recognised values: fte1 fte2 csqc.

name: sv_playerslots
description: Specify maximum number of player/spectator/bot slots, new value takes effect on the next map (this may result in players getting kicked). This should generally be set to maxclients+maxspectators. Leave blank for a default value. Maximum value of

name: maxclients
description: Specify the maximum number of players allowed on the server at once. Can be changed mid-map.

name: maxspectators
description: Specify the maximum number of spectators allowed on the server at once. Can be changed mid-map.

name: allow_skybox
description: This setting says whether clients should skip writing skybox depth when rendering skyboxes/skydomes. Skipping depth writes is required for halflife, quake2, and quake3 compat, but q1 content generally requires depth masking. Empty uses format-specific defaults.

name: allow_splitscreen
description: Specifies whether clients can use splitscreen extensions to dynamically add additional clients. This only affects remote clients and not the built-in client. Clients may need to reconnect in order to add seats when this is changed.

name: filterban
description: If 0, players will be kicked by default unless there is a rule that allows them. Also affects the default action of addip.

name: sv_savefmt
description: Specifies the format used for the saved game. 0=legacy. 1=fte 2=binary

name: sv_autosave
description: Interval for autosaves, in minutes. Set to 0 to disable autosave.

name: sv_heartbeattimeout
description: How many seconds a server should remain listed after its latest heartbeat. Larger values can avoid issues from packetloss, but can also make dos attacks easier.

name: sv_maxgames
description: Limits the number of games that may be known. This is to reduce denial of service attacks.

name: sv_maxservers
description: Limits the number of servers (total from all games) that may be known. This is to reduce denial of service attacks.

name: sv_hideinactivegames
description: Don't show known games that currently have no servers in html listings.

name: sv_sortlist
description: Controls sorting of the http output: 0: don't bother 1: clients then address 2: hostname then address 3: clients then hostname then address 4: just address

name: hostname
description: Controls sorting of the http output: 0: don't bother 1: clients then address 2: hostname then address 3: clients then hostname then address 4: just address

name: sv_slaverequery
description: Requery slave masters at this frequency.

name: sv_qwmasterslave1
description: Specifies a different (quakeworld-protocol) master from which to steal server listings.

name: sv_qwmasterslave2
description: Specifies a different (quakeworld-protocol) master from which to steal server listings.

name: sv_qwmasterslave3
description: Specifies a different (quakeworld-protocol) master from which to steal server listings.

name: sv_q2masterslave1
description: Specifies a different (quake2-protocol) master from which to steal server listings.

name: sv_q2masterslave2
description: Specifies a different (quake2-protocol) master from which to steal server listings.

name: sv_q2masterslave3
description: Specifies a different (quake2-protocol) master from which to steal server listings.

name: sv_q3masterslave1
description: Specifies a different (quake3-protocol) master from which to steal server listings.

name: sv_q3masterslave2
description: Specifies a different (quake3-protocol) master from which to steal server listings.

name: sv_q3masterslave3
description: Specifies a different (quake3-protocol) master from which to steal server listings.

name: sv_dpmasterslave1
description: Specifies a different (dpmaster-protocol) master from which to steal server listings.

name: sv_dpmasterslave2
description: Specifies a different (dpmaster-protocol) master from which to steal server listings.

name: sv_dpmasterslave3
description: Specifies a different (dpmaster-protocol) master from which to steal server listings.

name: sv_aim
description: The value should be cos(angle), where angle is the greatest allowed angle from which to deviate from the direction the shot would have been along. Values >1 thus disable auto-aim. This can be overridden with setinfo aim 0.73.

name: sv_maxaim
description: The maximum acceptable angle for autoaiming.

name: pr_ssqc_memsize
description: The ammount of memory available to the QC vm. This has a theoretical maximum of 1gb, but that value can only really be used in 64bit builds. -1 will attempt to use some conservative default, but you may need to increase it. Consider also clearing pr_fixbrokenqccarrays if you need to change this cvar.

name: pr_imitatemvdsv
description: Enables mvdsv-specific builtins, and fakes identifiers so that mods made for mvdsv can run properly and with the full feature set.

name: pr_maxedicts
description: Maximum number of entities spawnable on the map at once. Low values will crash the server on some maps/mods. High values will result in excessive memory useage (see pr_ssqc_memsize). Illegible server messages may occur with old/other clients above 32k. FTE's network protocols have a maximum at a little over 4 million. Please don't ever make a mod that actually uses that many...

name: pr_no_playerphysics
description: Prevents support of the 'SV_PlayerPhysics' QC function. This allows servers to prevent needless breakage of player prediction.

name: pr_no_parsecommand
description: Provides a way around invalid mod usage of SV_ParseClientCommand, eg xonotic.

name: sv_progs
description: Specifies the filename of the gamecode to use serverside. Empty means autodetect - typically loading either progs or qwprogs depending on gamedir priority, then based upon deathmatch settings.

name: pr_nonetaccess
description: Block all direct access to network buffers (the writebyte builtin and friends will ignore the call).

name: pr_compatabilitytest
description: Only enables builtins if the extension they are part of was queried.

name: sv_gameplayfix_setmodelrealbox
description: Vanilla setmodel will setsize the entity to a hardcoded size for non-bsp models. This cvar will always use the real size of the model instead, but will require that the server actually loads the model.

name: sv_gameplayfix_setmodelsize_qw
description: The setmodel builtin will act as a setsize for QuakeWorld mods also.

name: dpcompat_nopreparse
description: Xonotic uses svc_tempentity with unknowable lengths mixed with other data that needs to be translated. This cvar disables any attempt to translate or pre-parse network messages, including disabling nq/qw cross compatibility. NOTE: because preparsing will be disabled, messages might not get backbuffered correctly if too much reliable data is written.

name: dpcompat_precachesoundhack
description: Changes the behaviour of only the ssqc's precache_sound to return a precache index. precache_model and csqc's precaches are unchanged.

name: dpcompat_traceontouch
description: Report trace plane etc when an entity touches another.

name: pr_ext_dp_qc_getsurface
description: Set to 0 to block detection of the extension

name: sv_fullredirect
description: This is the ip:port to redirect players to when the server is full

name: sv_antilag
description: Attempt to backdate impacts to compensate for lag via the MOVE_ANTILAG feature. 0=completely off. 1=mod-controlled (default). 2=forced, which might break certain uses of traceline. 3=Also attempt to recalculate trace start positions to avoid lagged knockbacks.

name: sv_showpredloss
description: Print messages whenever input frames are ignored or forced serverside, to prevent speedcheats or hover cheats. Any such prints will be accompanied by prediction misses in the named client.

name: sv_cheatpc
description: If the client tried to claim more than this percentage of time within any speed-cheat period, the client will be deemed to have cheated.

name: sv_cheatspeedchecktime
description: The interval between each speed-cheat check.

name: sv_ping_ignorepl
description: If 1, ping times reported for players will ignore the effects of packetloss on ping times. 0 is slightly more honest, but less useful for connection diagnosis.

name: sv_protocol_nq
description: Specifies the default protocol to use for new NQ clients. This is only relevent for clients that do not report their supported protocols. Supported values are 0 = autodetect 15 = vanilla 666 = fitzquake 999 = rmq protocol The sv_bigcoords cvar forces upgrades as required.

name: minpitch
description: Assumed to be -70

name: maxpitch
description: Assumed to be 80

name: sv_pure
description: The most evil cvar in the world, many clients will ignore this. 0=standard quake rules. 1=clients should prefer files within packages present on the server. 2=clients should use *only* files within packages present on the server. Due to quake 1.01/1.06 differences, a setting of 2 only works in total conversions.

name: sv_brokenmovetypes
description: Emulate vanilla quakeworld by forcing MOVETYPE_WALK on all players. Shouldn't be used for any games other than QuakeWorld.

name: pext_ezquake_nochunks
description: Prevents ezquake clients from being able to use the chunked download extension. This sidesteps numerous ezquake issues, and will make downloads slower but more robust.

name: pext_ezquake_verfortrans
description: ezQuake does not implement PEXT_TRANS properly. This is the version of ezquake required for PEXT_TRANS to be allowed. This was still broken when I wrote this description, hence the large value.

name: votelevel
description: This is the restriction level of commands that players may vote for. You can reconfigure commands, cvars, or aliases individually. Additionally, aliases can be configured via aliaslevel to be executed at a different level from their restriction level. This can be used to indirectly allow voting for 'map dm4' for instance, without allowing people to vote for every map.

name: voteminimum
description: At least this many players must vote the same way for the vote to pass.

name: votepercent
description: At least this percentage of players must vote the same way for the vote to pass.

name: votetime
description: Votes will be discarded after this many minutes

name: pr_allowbutton1
description: The button1 field is believed to have been intended to work with the +use command, but it was never hooked up. In NetQuake, this field was often repurposed for other things as it was not otherwise used (and cannot be removed without breaking the crc), while third-party QuakeWorld engines did decide to implement it as believed was intended. As a result, this cvar only applies to QuakeWorld mods and a value of 1 is only likely to cause issues with NQ mods that were ported to QW.

name: sv_getrealip
description: Attempt to obtain a more reliable IP for clients, rather than just their proxy. 0: Don't attempt. 1: Unreliable checks. 2: Validate if possible. 3: Mandatory validation.

name: sv_realip_kick
description: Kicks clients if their realip could not be validated to the level specified by sv_getrealip.

name: sv_realiphostname_ipv4
description: This is the server's public ip:port. This is needed for realip to work when the autodetected/local ip is not globally routable

name: sv_realiphostname_ipv6
description: This is the server's public ip:port. This is needed for realip to work when the autodetected/local ip is not globally routable

name: sv_userinfo_keylimit
description: This is the maximum number of userinfo keys each user may create.

name: sv_userinfo_bytelimit
description: This is the maximum number of bytes that may be stored into each user's userinfo. Note that this includes key names too.

name: sv_voip
description: Enable reception of voice packets.

name: sv_voip_record
description: Record voicechat into mvds. Requires player support. 0=noone, 1=everyone, 2=spectators only

name: sv_voip_echo
description: Echo voice packets back to their sender, a debug/test setting.

name: sv_writelevel
description: Specifies the required trust level at which user accounts may write to the user-specific subdir of /uploads/USERNAME/*. If blank, then no uploads are permitted

name: sv_fulllevel
description: User accounts with an access level greater than this may write anywhere, including the gamedir. Note that setting this low is increadibly risky. An empty value will be understood to never give this permission.

name: sv_ftp_port_range
description: Specifies the port range for the server to create listening sockets for 'active' ftp connections, to work around NAT/firewall issues. Most FTP clients should use passive connections, but there's still some holdouts like windows.

name: ConsoleName
description: Description

name: q3bsp_surf_meshcollision_flag
description: The surfaceparm flag(s) that enables q3bsp trisoup collision

name: q3bsp_surf_meshcollision_force
description: Force mesh-based collisions on all q3bsp trisoup surfaces.

name: q3bsp_mergelightmaps
description: Specifies whether to merge lightmaps into atlases in order to boost performance. Unfortunately this breaks tcgen on lightmap passes - if you care, set this to 0.

name: q3bsp_ignorestyles
description: Ignores multiple lightstyles in Raven's q3bsp variant(and derivatives) for better batch/rendering performance.

name: _q3bsp_bihtraces
description: Uses runtime-generated bih collision culling for faster traces.

name: map_autoopenportals
description: When set to 1, force-opens all area portals. Normally these start closed and are opened by doors when they move, but this requires the gamecode to signal this.

name: fs_hidesyspaths
description: 0: Show system paths in console prints that might appear in screenshots or video capture. 1: Replace the start of filenames in console prints with generic prefixes that cannot leak private info like your operating system's user name.

name: fs_cache
description: 0: Do individual lookups. 1: Scan all files for accelerated lookups. This provides a performance boost on windows and avoids case sensitivity issues on linux. 2: like 1, but don't bother checking for external changes (avoiding the cost of rebuild the cache).

name: fs_noreexec
description: Disables automatic re-execing configs on gamedir switches. This means your cvar defaults etc may be from the wrong mod, and cfg_save will leave that stuff corrupted!

name: fs_gamepath
description: Provided for Q2/Q3 compat. System path of the active gamedir.

name: fs_basepath
description: Provided for Q2/Q3 compat. System path of the base directory.

name: fs_homepath
description: Provided for Q2/Q3 compat. System path of the base directory.

name: fs_packageprioritisation
description: Favours the package that is: 0: Most recently modified 1: Is alphabetically last (favour z over a, 9 over 0).

name: sv_dlURL
description: Provides clients with an external url from which they can obtain pk3s/packages from an external http server instead of having to download over udp.

name: cl_download_mapsrc
description: Specifies an http location prefix for map downloads. EG: "http://example.com/path/gamemaps/"

name: net_ice_broker
description: This is the default broker we attempt to connect through when using 'sv_public /foo' or 'connect /foo'.

name: timeout
description: Connections will time out if no packets are received for this duration of time.

name: net_hybriddualstack
description: Uses hybrid ipv4+ipv6 sockets where possible. Not supported on xp or below.

name: net_fakeloss
description: Simulates packetloss in both receiving and sending, on a scale from 0 to 1.

name: net_fakemtu
description: Cripples packet reception sizes.

name: net_dns_ipv4
description: If 0, disables dns resolution of names to ipv4 addresses (removing any associated error messages). Also hides ipv4 addresses in address:port listings.

name: net_dns_ipv6
description: If 0, disables dns resolution of names to ipv6 addresses (removing any associated error messages). Also hides ipv6 addresses in address:port listings.

name: net_enabled
description: If 0, disables all network access, including name resolution and socket creation. Does not affect loopback/internal connections.

name: tls_ignorecertificateerrors
description: This should NEVER be set to 1!

name: net_enable_qizmo
description: Enables serverside support for 'connect tcp://foo' or 'connect tls://foo' (with net_enable_tls), as well as qizmo's tcp connections and compatibles.

name: net_enable_qtv
description: Controls whether inbound qtv requests will be honoured (both proxies and clients using qtvplay). 0: Don't accept qtv connections. 1: Accept connections. 2: Accept qtv connections only from host-scopped addresses (read: 127.*.*.*, ::1, or unix sockets).

name: net_enable_tls
description: If enabled, binary data sent to a non-tls tcp port will be interpretted as a tls handshake (enabling https or wss over the same tcp port.

name: net_enable_http
description: If enabled, tcp ports will accept inbound http clients, potentially serving large files which could distrupt gameplay (This does not affect outgoing http(s) requests).

name: net_enable_rtcbroker
description: If 1, tcp ports will accept websocket connections from clients trying to broker direct webrtc connections. This should be low traffic, but might involve a lot of mostly-idle connections.

name: net_enable_websockets
description: If enabled, tcp ports will accept websocket game clients.

name: dtls_psk_hint
description: For DTLS-PSK handshakes. This specifies the public server identity.

name: dtls_psk_user
description: For DTLS-PSK handshakes. This specifies the username to use when the client+server's hints match.

name: dtls_psk_key
description: For DTLS-PSK handshakes. This specifies the hexadecimal key which must match between client+server. Will only be used when client+server's hint settings match.

name: developer
description: Enables the spewing of additional developer/debugging messages. 2 will give even more spam, much of it unwanted.

name: registered
description: Set if quake's pak1.pak is available

name: gameversion
description: gamecode version for server browsers

name: gameversion_min
description: gamecode version for server browsers

name: gameversion_max
description: gamecode version for server browsers

name: pr_engine
description: This cvar exists so that the menuqc is able to determine which engine-specific settings/values to list/suggest. It must not be used to detect formal QC extensions/builtins. Use checkextension/checkbuiltin/checkcommand for that.

name: com_fullgamename
description: The filesystem is trying to run this game

name: com_protocolname
description: The protocol game name used for dpmaster queries. For compatibility with DP, you can set this to 'DarkPlaces-Quake' in order to be listed in DP's master server, and to list DP servers.

name: com_protocolversion
description: The protocol version used for dpmaster queries.

name: com_parseutf8
description: Interpret console messages/playernames/etc as UTF-8. Requires special fonts. -1=iso 8859-1. 0=quakeascii(chat uses high chars). 1=utf8, revert to ascii on decode errors. 2=utf8 ignoring errors

name: com_highlightcolor
description: ANSI colour to be used for highlighted text, used when com_parseutf8 is active.

name: com_gamedirnativecode
description: FULLENGINENAME" blocks all downloads of files with a .dll or .so extension

name: mapname
description: Cvar that holds the short name of the current map, for scripting type stuff

name: ezcompat_markup
description: Attempt compatibility with ezquake's text markup.0: disabled. 1: Handle markup ampersand markup. 2: Handle chevron markup (only in echo commands, for config compat, because its just too unreliable otherwise).

name: pm_noround
description: Disables player prediction snapping, in a way that cannot be reliably predicted but may be needed to avoid map bugs.

name: scr_usekfont
description: Exists for compat with the quake rerelease, changing the behaviour of QC's sprint/bprint/centerprint builtins.

name: worker_flush
description: If set, process the entire load queue, loading stuff faster but at the risk of stalling the main thread.

name: worker_sleeptime
description: Causes workers to sleep for a period of time after each job.

name: net_mtu
description: Specifies a maximum udp payload size, above which packets will be fragmented. If routers all worked properly this could be some massive value, and some massive value may work really nicely for lans. Use smaller values than the default if you're connecting through nested tunnels through routers that fail with IP fragmentation.

name: net_compress
description: Enables huffman compression of network packets.

name: _pext_vrinputs
description: RENAME ME WHEN STABLE. Networks player inputs slightly differently, allowing for greater capabilities, particuarly vr controller info.

name: _pext_lerptime
description: RENAME ME WHEN STABLE. Sends timing hints for interpolation.

name: _pext_infoblobs
description: RENAME ME WHEN STABLE. Enables the use of very large infokeys containing potentially invalid chars. Note that the userinfo is still limited by sv_userinfo_bytelimit and sv_userinfo_keylimit.

name: pext_replacementdeltas
description: Enables the use of alternative nack-based entity deltas

name: pext_predinfo
description: Enables some extra things to support prediction over NQ protocols.

name: log_readable
description: Bitfield describing what to convert/strip. If 0, exact byte representation will be used. &1: Dequakify text. &2: Strip special markup. &4: Strip ansi control codes.

name: log_developer
description: Enables logging of console prints when set to 1. Otherwise unimportant messages will not fill up your log files.

name: ipautodump
description: Enables dumping the 'iplog.txt' file, which contains a log of usernames seen for a given IP, which is useful for detecting fake-nicks.

name: mod_h2holey_bugged
description: Hexen2's holey-model flag uses index 0 as transparent (and additionally 255 in gl, due to a bug). GLQuake engines tend to have bugs that use ONLY index 255, resulting in a significant compatibility issue that can be resolved only with this shitty cvar hack.

name: mod_halftexel
description: Offset texture coords by a half-texel, for compatibility with glquake and the majority of engine forks.

name: mod_nomipmap
description: Disables the use of mipmaps on quake1 mdls, consistent with its original software renderer.

name: mod_obj_orientation
description: Controls how the model's axis are interpreted. 0: x=forward, z=up (Quake) 1: x=forward, y=up 2: z=forward, y=up

name: mod_md5_singleanimation
description: When loading an md5mesh file, also attempt to load an .md5anim file, and unpack it into individual poses. Use 0 for mods that will be precaching their own md5anims for use with skeletal objects.

name: dpcompat_skinfiles
description: When set, uses a nodraw shader for any unmentioned surfaces.

name: dpcompat_nofloodfill
description: Disables the q1mdl floodfill. Setting this to 1 may result in blue seams on the vanilla quake models.

name: plug_sbar
description: Controls whether plugins are allowed to draw the hud, rather than the engine (when allowed by csqc). This is typically used to permit the ezhud plugin without needing to bother unloading it. =0: never use hud plugins. &1: Use hud plugins in deathmatch. &2: Use hud plugins in singleplayer/coop. =3: Always use hud plugins (when loaded).

name: plug_loaddefault
description: 0: Load plugins only via explicit plug_load commands 1: Load built-in plugins and those selected via the package manager 2: Scan for misc plugins, loading all that can be found, but not built-ins. 3: Scan for plugins, and then load any built-ins

name: utf8_enable
description: When 1, changes the qc builtins to act upon codepoints instead of bytes. Do not use unless com_parseutf8 is also set.

name: sv_gameplayfix_nolinknonsolid
description: When 0, setorigin et al will not link the entity into the collision nodes (which is faster, especially if you have a lot of non-solid entities. When 1, allows entities to freely switch between .solid values (except for SOLID_BSP) without relinking. A lot of DP mods assume a value of 1 and will bug out otherwise, while 0 will restore a bugs present in various mods.

name: sv_gameplayfix_blowupfallenzombies
description: Allow findradius to find non-solid entities. This may break certain mods. It is better for mods to use FL_FINDABLE_NONSOLID instead.

name: sv_gameplayfix_findradiusdistancetobox
description: When 1, findradius checks to the nearest part of the entity instead of only its origin, making it find slightly more entities.

name: sv_gameplayfix_droptofloorstartsolid
description: When droptofloor fails, this causes a second attemp, but with traceline instead.

name: dpcompat_findradiusarealinks
description: Use the world collision info to accelerate findradius instead of looping through every single entity. May actually be slower for large radiuses, or fail to find entities which have not been linked properly with setorigin.

name: dpcompat_strcat_limit
description: When set, cripples strcat (and related function) string lengths to the value specified. Set to 16383 to replicate DP's limit, otherwise leave as 0 to avoid limits.

name: pr_autocreatecvars
description: Implicitly create any cvars that don't exist when read.

name: pr_droptofloorunits
description: Distance that droptofloor is allowed to drop to be considered successul.

name: pr_fixbrokenqccarrays
description: As part of its nq/qw/h2/csqc support, FTE remaps QC fields to match an internal order. This is a faster way to handle extended fields. However, some QCCs are buggy and don't report all field defs. 0: do nothing. QCC must be well behaved. 1: Duplicate engine fields, remap the ones we can to known offsets. This is sufficient for QCCX/FrikQCC mods that use hardcoded or even occasional calculated offsets (fixes ktpro). 2: Scan the mod for field accessing instructions, and assume those are the fields (and that they don't alias non-fields). This can be used to work around gmqcc's WTFs (fixes xonotic).

name: pr_tempstringcount
description: Obsolete. Set to 16 if you want to recycle+reuse the same 16 tempstring references and break lots of mods.

name: pr_tempstringsize
description: Obsolete

name: pr_gc_threaded
description: Says whether to use a separate thread for tempstring garbage collections. This avoids main-thread stalls but at the expense of more memory usage.

name: pr_sourcedir
description: Subdirectory where your qc source is located. Used by the internal compiler and qc debugging functionality.

name: pr_enable_uriget
description: Allows gamecode to make direct http requests

name: pr_enable_profiling
description: Enables profiling support. Will run more slowly. Change the map and then use the profile_ssqc/profile_csqc commands to see the results.

name: pr_precachepic_slow
description: Legacy setting. Should be set to 0 where supported.

name: net_ice_exchangeprivateips
description: Boolean. When set to 0, hides private IP addresses from your peers - only addresses determined from the other side of your router will be shared. You should only need to set this to 1 if mdns is unavailable.

name: net_ice_allowstun
description: Boolean. When set to 0, prevents the use of stun to determine our public address (does not prevent connecting to our peer's server-reflexive candidates).

name: net_ice_allowturn
description: Boolean. When set to 0, prevents registration of turn connections (does not prevent connecting to our peer's relay candidates).

name: net_ice_allowmdns
description: Boolean. When set to 0, prevents the use of multicast-dns to obtain candidates using random numbers instead of revealing private network info.

name: net_ice_relayonly
description: Boolean. When set to 1, blocks reporting non-relay local candidates, does not attempt to connect to remote candidates other than via a relay.

name: net_ice_usewebrtc
description: Use webrtc's extra overheads rather than simple ICE. This makes packets larger and is slower to connect, but is compatible with the web port.

name: net_ice_servers
description: A space-separated list of ICE servers, eg stun:host.example:3478 or turn:host.example:3478?user=foo?auth=blah

name: net_ice_debug
description: 0: Hide messy details. 1: Show new candidates. 2: Show each connectivity test.

name: dpcompat_console
description: Enables hacks to emulate DP's console.

name: cfg_save_all
description: If 1, cfg_save ALWAYS saves all cvars. If 0, cfg_save only ever saves archived cvars. If empty, cfg_save saves all cvars only when an explicit filename was given (ie: when not used internally via quit menu options).

name: cfg_save_auto
description: If 1, the config will automatically be saved and without prompts. If 0, you'll have to save your config manually (possibly via prompts from the quit menu).

name: cfg_save_infos
description: If 1, saves userinfo and serverinfo to configs.

name: cfg_save_aliases
description: If 1, saves aliases to configs. Note that aliases sent from servers are assumed to be server-specific and are never saved (and are forgotten on map changes, too).

name: cfg_save_binds
description: If 1, saves all key bindings to configs.

name: cfg_save_buttons
description: If 1, saves the state of things such as +mlook or +forward to configs.

name: cl_aliasoverlap
description: Rename new aliases if they would override cvar names.

name: sys_osk
description: Enables support for text input. This will be ignored when the console has focus, but gamecode may end up with composition boxes appearing.

name: joyonly
description: If true, treats "game controllers" as regular joysticks. Must be set at startup.

name: cl_movement
description: Specifies whether to send movement sequence info over DPP7 protocols (other protocols are unaffected). Unlike cl_nopred, this can result in different serverside behaviour.

name: cl_c2sdupe
description: Send duplicate copies of packets to the server. This avoids extra latency caused by packetloss, but could also make the problem worse.

name: cl_c2spps
description: Reduces outgoing packet rates by dropping up to a third of outgoing packets.

name: cl_c2sImpulseBackup
description: Prevents the cl_c2spps setting from dropping redundant packets that contain impulses, in an attempt to keep impulses more reliable.

name: cl_c2sMaxRedundancy
description: This is the maximum number of input frames to send in each input packet. Values greater than 1 provide redundancy and avoid prediction misses, though you might find cl_c2sdupe provides equivelent result and at lower latency. It is locked at 3 for vanilla quakeworld, and locked at 1 for vanilla netquake.

name: cl_netfps
description: Send up to this many packets to the server per second. The rate used is also limited by the server which usually forces a cap to this setting of 77. Low packet rates can result in extra extrapolation to try to hide the resulting latencies.

name: cl_queueimpulses
description: Queues unsent impulses instead of replacing them. This avoids the need for extra wait commands (and the timing issues of such commands), but potentially increases latency and can cause scripts to be desynced with regard to buttons and impulses.

name: cl_smartjump
description: Makes the jump button act as +moveup when in water. This is typically quieter and faster.

name: cl_iDrive
description: Effectively releases movement keys when the opposing key is pressed. This avoids dead-time when both keys are pressed. This can be emulated with various scripts, but that's messy.

name: cl_run
description: Enables autorun, inverting the state of the +speed key.

name: cl_fastaccel
description: Begin moving at full speed instantly, instead of waiting a frame or so.

name: cl_sendchatstate
description: Announce your chat state to the server in a privacy-violating kind of way. This allows other players to see your afk/at-console status.

name: in_vraim
description: When set to 1, the 'view' angle sent to the server is controlled by your vr headset instead of separately. This is for fallback behaviour and blocks mouse+joy+gamepad aiming.

name: in_forceseat
description: Overrides the device identifiers to control a specific client from any device. This can be used for debugging mods, where you only have one keyboard/mouse.

name: cl_weaponhide
description: HACK: Attempt to switch weapon to another in order to cheat your killer out of any possible weapon upgrades. 0: original behaviour 1: switch away when +attack/+fire is released 2: switch away only in deathmatch

name: cl_weaponhide_preference
description: The weapon you would like to try to switch to when cl_weaponhide is active

name: cl_weaponpreselect
description: HACK: Controls the interaction between the ^aweapon^a and ^a+attack^a commands (does not affect ^aimpulse^a). 0: weapon switch happens instantly 1: weapon switch happens on next attack 2: instant only when already firing, otherwise delayed 3: delay until new attack only in deathmatch 1 4: delay until any attack only in deathmatch 1

name: cl_weaponforgetorder
description: The 'weapon' command will lock in its weapon choice, instead of choosing a different weapon between select+fire.

name: r_viewpreselgun
description: HACK: Display the preselected weaponmodel, instead of the current weaponmodel.

name: cl_backspeed
description: The base speed that you move backwards at. If empty, uses the value of cl_forwardspeed instead.

name: cl_predict_extrapolate
description: If 1, enables prediction based upon partial input frames which can change over time resulting in a swimmy feel but does not need to interpolate. If 0, prediction will stay in the past and thus use only completed frames. Interpolation will then be used to smooth movement. This cvar only applies when video and input frames are independant (ie: cl_netfps is set).

name: cl_predict_timenudge
description: A debug feature. You should normally leave this as 0. Nudges local player prediction into the future if positive (resulting in extrapolation), or into the past if negative (resulting in laggy interpolation). Value is in seconds, so small decimals are required. This cvar applies even if input frames are tied to video frames.

name: cl_lerp_smooth
description: If 2, will act as 1 when playing demos/singleplayer and otherwise act as if set to 0 (ie: deathmatch). If 1, interpolation will run in the past, resulting in really smooth movement at the cost of latency (even on bunchy german ISDNs). If 0, interpolation will be based upon packet arrival times and may judder due to packet loss.

name: cl_lerp_driftbias
description: Additional bias, can be set to a negative value to hold interpolation in the past.

name: cl_lerp_driftfrac
description: Proportion of the latest time vs the older time to favour drifting towards.

name: cl_nopred
description: Disables clientside movement prediction.

name: gl_nocolors
description: Ignores player colours and skins, reducing texture memory usage at the cost of not knowing whether you're killing your team mates.

name: gl_part_flame
description: Enable particle emitting from models. Mainly used for torch and flame effects.

name: gl_driver
description: Specifies the graphics driver name to load. This is typically a filename. Blank for default.

name: vid_devicename
description: Specifies which video device to try to use. If blank or invalid then one will be guessed.

name: gl_shadeq1_name
description: Rename all surfaces from quake1 bsps using this pattern for the purposes of shader names.

name: r_ignoreentpvs
description: Disables pvs culling of entities that have been submitted to the renderer.

name: mod_md3flags
description: The flags field of md3s was never officially defined. If this is set to 1, the flags will be treated identically to mdl files. Otherwise they will be ignored. Naturally, this is required to provide rotating pickups in quake.

name: r_bouncysparks
description: Enables particle interaction with world surfaces, allowing for bouncy particles, stains, and decals.

name: r_drawentities
description: Controls whether to draw entities or not. 0: Draw no entities. 1: Draw everything as normal. 2: Draw everything but bmodels. 3: Draw bmodels only.

name: r_max_gpu_bones
description: Specifies the maximum number of bones that can be handled on the GPU. If empty, will guess.

name: r_showtris
description: Developer feature where everything is drawn with wireframe over the top. Only active where cheats are permitted.

name: gl_outline
description: Draw some stylised outlines.

name: gl_outline_width
description: The width of those outlines.

name: r_refract_fbo
description: Use an fbo for refraction. If 0, just renders as a portal and uses a copy of the current framebuffer.

name: r_refractreflect_scale
description: Use a different scale for refraction and reflection texturemaps. Because $reasons.

name: r_dynamic
description: 0: no standard dlights at all. 1: coloured dlights will be used, they may show through walls. These are not realtime things. 2: The dlights will be forced to monochrome (this does not affect coronas/flashblends/rtlights attached to the same light).

name: r_fb_bmodels
description: Enables loading lumas on the map, as well as any external bsp models.

name: r_fb_models
description: Enables the use of lumas on models. Note that if ruleset_allow_fbmodels is enabled, then all models are unconditionally fullbright in deathmatch, because cheaters would set up their models like that anyway, hurrah for beating them at their own game. QuakeWorld players suck.

name: gl_overbright_models
description: Doubles the brightness of models, to match QuakeSpasm's misfeature of the same name.

name: r_globalskin_first
description: Specifies the first .skin value that is a global skin. Entities within this range will use the shader/image called 'gfx/skinSKIN.lmp' instead of their regular skin. See also: r_globalskin_count.

name: r_globalskin_count
description: Specifies how many globalskins there are.

name: r_coronas
description: Draw coronas on realtime lights. Overrides glquake-esque flashblends.

name: r_coronas_intensity
description: Alternative intensity multiplier for coronas.

name: r_coronas_occlusion
description: Specifies that coronas should be occluded more carefully. 0: No occlusion, at all. 1: BSP occlusion only (simple tracelines). 2: non-bsp occlusion also (complex tracelines). 3: Depthbuffer reads (forces synchronisation). 4: occlusion queries.

name: r_coronas_mindist
description: Coronas closer than this will be invisible, preventing near clip plane issues.

name: r_coronas_fadedist
description: Coronas will fade out over this distance.

name: r_fullbright
description: Ignore world lightmaps, drawing *everything* fully lit.

name: r_fullbrightSkins
description: Force the use of fullbright skins on other players. No more hiding in the dark.

name: r_shadow_realtime_nonworld_lightmaps
description: Scaler for lightmaps used when not using realtime world lighting. Probably broken.

name: r_loadlit
description: Whether to load lit files. 0: Do not load external rgb lightmap data. 1: Load but don't generate. 2: Generate ldr lighting (if none found). 3: Generate hdr lighting (if none found). Note that regeneration of lightmap data may be unreliable if the map was made for more advanced lighting tools. Deluxemap information will also be generated, as appropriate.

name: r_netgraph
description: Displays a graph of packet latency. A value of 2 will give additional info about what sort of data is being received from the server.

name: r_part_rain
description: Enable particle effects to emit off of surfaces. Mainly used for weather or lava/slime effects.

name: r_softwarebanding
description: Utilise the Quake colormap in order to emulate 8bit software rendering. This results in banding as well as other artifacts that some believe adds character. Also forces nearest sampling on affected surfaces (palette indicies do not interpolate well).

name: r_renderscale
description: Provides a way to enable subsampling or super-sampling

name: r_fxaa
description: Runs a post-procesing pass to strip the jaggies.

name: r_graphics
description: Turning this off will result in ascii-style rendering.

name: r_postprocshader
description: Specifies a custom shader to use as a post-processing shader

name: r_waterwarp
description: Enables fullscreen warp, preferably via glsl. -1 specifies to force the fov warp fallback instead which can give a smidge more performance.

name: r_replacemodels
description: A list of filename extensions to attempt to use instead of mdl.

name: gl_lightmap_nearest
description: Use nearest sampling for lightmaps. This will give a more blocky look. Meaningless when gl_lightmap_average is enabled.

name: gl_lightmap_average
description: Determine lightmap values based upon the center of the polygon. This will give a more buggy look, quite probably.

name: scr_allowsnap
description: Whether to allow server-requested screenshot requests to check for gpu driver hacks.

name: scr_centertime
description: Centerprint messages will be displayed for this long before disappearing.

name: con_logcenterprint
description: Specifies whether to print centerprints on the console. 0: never 1: single-player or coop only. 2: always.

name: scr_consize
description: Proportion of the screen to be covered by the console when its focused. Valid range is 0ish to 1.

name: scr_conspeed
description: How fast the console careers onto the screen.

name: scr_fov_mode
description: Controls what the fov cvar actually controls: 0: largest axis (ultra-wide monitors means less height will be visible). 1: smallest axis (ultra-wide monitors will distort at the edges). 2: horizontal axis. 3: vertical axis. 4: 4:3 horizontal axis, padded for wider resolutions (for a more classic fov)

name: r_viewmodel_fov
description: field of vision, 1-170 degrees, standard fov is 90, nquake defaults to 108.

name: scr_printspeed
description: How rapidly each character is displayed during outro messages.

name: showturtle
description: Enables a low-framerate indicator.

name: scr_sshot_compression
description: Requsted compression ratio as a percentage. For jpeg this is the quantisation quality and has a direct impact on image quality vs size. For png this ranges between 0 for best and 100 for worst with final image quality being unchanged because png is loseless.

name: scr_sshot_type
description: This specifies the default extension(and thus file format) for screenshots. Known extensions are: png, jpg/jpeg, bmp, pcx, tga, ktx, dds.

name: vid_conautoscale
description: Changes the 2d scale, including hud, console, and fonts. To specify an explicit font size, divide the desired 'point' size by 8 to get the scale. High values will be clamped to maintain at least a 320*200 virtual size.

name: vid_baseheight
description: Specifies a mod's target height and used only when the 2d scale is not otherwise forced. Unlike vid_conheight the size is not fixed and will be padded to avoid inconsistent filtering.

name: vid_minsize
description: Specifies a mod's minimum virtual size (to be set inside the mod's default.cfg file). The virtual size will always be at least this big. Windows may not be resized below this value either (which may reduce video mode options), but might require a vid_restart to take effect when switching between mods.

name: vid_conheight
description: Specifies the virtual height of the screen. If 0, will be inferred by vid_conwidth or other settings, according to aspect etc.

name: vid_conwidth
description: Specifies the virtual width of the screen. Should generally be left as 0 to allow the correct aspect to be used despite video mode changes.

name: vid_renderer
description: Specifies which backend is used. Values that might work are: sv (dedicated server), headless (null renderer), vk (vulkan), gl (opengl), egl (opengl es), d3d9 (direct3d 9), d3d11 (direct3d 11, with default hardware rendering), d3d11 warp (direct3d 11, with software rendering).

name: _vid_renderer_opts
description: The possible video renderer apis, in "value" "description" pairs, for gamecode to read.

name: vid_bpp
description: The number of colour bits to request from the renedering context

name: vid_depthbits
description: The number of depth bits to request from the renedering context. Try 24.

name: vid_desktopsettings
description: Ignore the values of vid_width and vid_height, and just use the same settings that are used for the desktop.

name: vid_fullscreen
description: Specifies whether the game should be fullscreen or not (requires vid_restart). 0: Run in a resizable window, which can be manually maximized (with borders). 1: Traditional fullscreen-exclusive video mode with mode switching and everything. 2: Simply maximize the window and hide any borders without interfering with any other parts of the system.

name: vid_height
description: The screen height to attempt to use, in physical pixels. 0 means use desktop resolution.

name: vid_multisample
description: The number of samples to use for Multisample AntiAliasing (aka: msaa). A value of 1 explicitly disables it.

name: vid_srgb
description: -1: Only the framebuffer should use sRGB colourspace, textures and colours will be assumed to be linear. This has the effect of just brightening the screen according to a gamma ramp of about 2.2 (or .45 in quake's backwards gamma terms). 0: Off. Colour blending will be wrong, you're likely to see obvious banding. 1: Use sRGB extensions/support to ensure that the lighting aproximately matches real-world lighting (required for PBR). 2: Attempt to use a linear floating-point framebuffer, which should enable monitor support for HDR. Note that driver behaviour varies by a disturbing amount, and much of the documentation conflicts with itself (the term 'linear' is awkward when the eye's perception of linear is non-linear).

name: vid_wndalpha
description: When running windowed, specifies the window's transparency level.

name: vid_winthread
description: When enabled, window messages will be handled by a separate thread. This allows the game to continue rendering when Microsoft Windows blocks while resizing, etc.

name: vid_width
description: The screen width to attempt to use, in physical pixels. 0 means use desktop resolution.

name: vid_dpi_x
description: For mods that need to determine the physical screen size (like with touchscreens). 0 means unknown

name: vid_dpi_y
description: For mods that need to determine the physical screen size (like with touchscreens). 0 means unknown

name: r_stereo_separation
description: How far apart your eyes are, in quake units. A non-zero value will enable stereoscoping rendering. You might need some of them retro 3d glasses. Hardware support is recommended, see r_stereo_context.

name: r_stereo_convergence
description: Nudges the angle of each eye inwards when using stereoscopic rendering.

name: r_stereo_method
description: Value 0 = Off. Value 1 = Attempt hardware acceleration. Requires vid_restart. Value 2 = red/cyan. Value 3 = red/blue. Value 4=red/green. Value 5=eye strain.

name: r_mirroralpha
description: Specifies how the default shader is generated for the 'window02_1' texture. Values less than 1 will turn it into a mirror.

name: r_polygonoffset_submodel_factor
description: z-fighting avoidance. Pushes submodel depth values slightly towards the camera depending on the slope of the surface.

name: r_polygonoffset_submodel_offset
description: z-fighting avoidance. Pushes submodel depth values slightly towards the camera by a consistent distance.

name: r_polygonoffset_submodel_map
description: List of maps on which z-fighting reduction should be used. wildcards accepted.

name: gl_workaround_ati_shadersource
description: Work around ATI driver bugs in the glShaderSource function. Can safely be enabled with other drivers too.

name: vid_gl_context_version
description: Specifies the version of OpenGL to try to create.

name: vid_gl_context_forwardcompatible
description: Requests an opengl context with no depricated features enabled.

name: vid_gl_context_compatibility
description: Requests an OpenGL context with fixed-function backwards compat.

name: vid_gl_context_debug
description: Requests a debug opengl context. This provides better error oreporting.

name: vid_gl_context_es
description: Requests an OpenGLES context. Be sure to set vid_gl_context_version to 2 or so.

name: vid_gl_context_robustness
description: Attempt to enforce extra buffer protection in the gl driver, but can be slower with pre-gl3 hardware.

name: vid_gl_context_selfreset
description: Upon hardware failure, have the engine create a new context instead of depending on the drivers to restore everything. This can help to avoid graphics drivers randomly killing your game, and can help reduce memory requirements.

name: vid_gl_context_noerror
description: Disables OpenGL's error checks for a small performance speedup. May cause segfaults if stuff wasn't properly implemented/tested.

name: gl_immutable_textures
description: Controls whether to use immutable GPU memory allocations for OpenGL textures. This potentially means less work for the drivers and thus higher framerates.

name: gl_immutable_buffers
description: Controls whether to use immutable GPU memory allocations for static OpenGL vertex buffers. This potentially means less work for the drivers and thus higher framerates.

name: gl_pbolightmaps
description: Controls whether to use PBOs for streaming lightmap updates. This prevents CPU stalls while the driver reads out the lightmap data (lightmap updates are still not free though).

name: r_tessellation
description: Enables+controls the use of blinn tessellation on the fallback shader for meshes, equivelent to a shader with 'program defaultskin#TESS'. This will look stupid unless the meshes were actually designed for it and have suitable vertex normals.

name: gl_blendsprites
description: Specifies how sprites are blended. 0: Alpha tested. 1: Premultiplied blend. 2: Additive blend.

name: r_deluxemapping
description: Enables bumpmapping based upon precomputed light directions. 0=off 1=use if available 2=auto-generate (if possible)

name: r_loadsurfenvmaps
description: Load local reflection environment-maps, where available. These are normally defined via env_cubemap entities dotted around the place.

name: r_shaderblobs
description: If enabled, can massively accelerate vid restarts / loading (especially with the d3d renderer). Can cause issues when upgrading engine versions, so this is disabled by default.

name: gl_compress
description: Enable automatic texture compression even for textures which are not pre-compressed.

name: gl_font
description: Specifies the font file to use. a value such as FONT:ALTFONT specifies an alternative font to be used when ^^a is used. When using TTF fonts, you will likely need to scale text to at least 150% - vid_conautoscale 1.5 will do this. TTF fonts may be loaded from your windows directory. \'gl_font cour?col=1,1,1:couri?col=0,1,0\' loads eg: c:\windows\fonts\cour.ttf, and uses the italic version of courier for alternative text, with specific colour tints.

name: con_textfont
description: Specifies the font file to use. a value such as FONT:ALTFONT specifies an alternative font to be used when ^^a is used. When using TTF fonts, you will likely need to scale text to at least 150% - vid_conautoscale 1.5 will do this. TTF fonts may be loaded from your windows directory. \'gl_font cour?col=1,1,1:couri?col=0,1,0\' loads eg: c:\windows\fonts\cour.ttf, and uses the italic version of courier for alternative text, with specific colour tints.

name: gl_lerpimages
description: Enables smoother resampling for images which are not power-of-two, when the drivers do not support non-power-of-two textures.

name: gl_max_size
description: Specifies the maximum texture size that the engine may use. Textures larger than this will be downsized. Clamped by the value the driver supports.

name: gl_menutint_shader
description: Controls the use of GLSL to desaturate the background when drawing the menu, like quake's dos software renderer used to do before the ugly dithering of winquake.

name: gl_mindist
description: Distance to the near clip plane. Smaller values may damage depth precision, high values can potentialy be used to see through walls...

name: gl_picmip
description: Reduce world/model texture sizes by some exponential factor.

name: gl_picmip2d
description: Reduce hud/menu texture sizes by some exponential factor.

name: gl_picmip_world
description: Effectively added to gl_picmip for the purposes of world textures.

name: gl_picmip_sprites
description: Effectively added to gl_picmip for the purposes of sprite textures.

name: gl_picmip_other
description: Effectively added to gl_picmip for the purposes of model textures.

name: gl_nohwblend
description: If 1, don't use hardware gamma ramps for transient effects that change each frame (does not affect long-term effects like holding quad or underwater tints).

name: gl_schematics
description: Gimmick rendering mode that draws the length of various world edges.

name: gl_maxdist
description: The distance of the far clip plane. If set to 0, some fancy maths will be used to place it at an infinite distance.

name: gl_specular
description: Multiplier for specular effects.

name: r_font_postprocess_outline
description: Controls the number of pixels of dark borders to use around fonts.

name: r_font_postprocess_mono
description: Disables anti-aliasing on fonts.

name: dpcompat_smallerfonts
description: Mimics DP's behaviour of using a smaller font size than was actually requested.

name: vid_triplebuffer
description: Specifies whether the hardware is forcing tripplebuffering on us, this is the number of extra page swaps required before old data has been completely overwritten.

name: r_portalrecursion
description: The number of portals the camera is allowed to recurse through.

name: r_portaldrawplanes
description: Draw front and back planes in portals. Debug feature.

name: r_portalonly
description: Don't draw things which are not portals. Debug feature.

name: r_lodscale
description: Scales the level-of-detail reduction on models (for those that have lod).

name: r_lodbias
description: Biases the level-of-detail on models (for those that have lod).

name: r_shadows
description: Draw basic blob shadows underneath entities without using realtime lighting.

name: r_showbboxes
description: Debugging. Shows bounding boxes. 1=ssqc, 2=csqc. Red=solid, Green=stepping/toss/bounce, Blue=onground.

name: r_showfields
description: Debugging. Shows entity fields boxes (entity closest to crosshair). 1=ssqc, 2=csqc, 3=snapshots.

name: r_showshaders
description: Debugging. Shows the name of the (worldmodel) shader being pointed to.

name: r_lightprepass
description: Experimental. Attempt to use a different lighting mechanism (aka: deferred lighting). Requires vid_reload to take effect.

name: r_shadow_bumpscale_basetexture
description: bumpyness scaler for generation of fallback normalmap textures from models

name: r_shadow_bumpscale_bumpmap
description: bumpyness scaler for _bump textures

name: r_shadow_heightscale_basetexture
description: scaler for generation of height maps from legacy paletted content.

name: r_shadow_heightscale_bumpmap
description: height scaler for 8bit _bump textures

name: r_glsl_pbr
description: Force PBR shading.

name: r_glsl_offsetmapping
description: Enables the use of paralax mapping, adding fake depth to textures.

name: r_glsl_offsetmapping_reliefmapping
description: Changes the paralax sampling mode to be a bit nicer, but noticably more expensive at high resolutions. r_glsl_offsetmapping must be set.

name: r_glsl_turbscale_reflect
description: Controls the strength of the water reflection ripples (used by the altwater glsl code).

name: r_glsl_turbscale_refract
description: Controls the strength of the underwater ripples (used by the altwater glsl code).

name: r_glsl_emissive
description: When set, specifies that the _luma or _glow textures are emissive... When 0 they are taken as a mask for the proportion of the lightmap that will apply (for q2e compat, has issues with overbrights).

name: r_fastturbcolour
description: The colour to use for water surfaces draw with r_waterstyle 0.

name: r_waterstyle
description: Changes how water, and teleporters are drawn. Possible values are: 0: fastturb-style block colour. 1: regular q1-style water. 2: refraction(ripply and transparent) 3: refraction with reflection at an angle 4: ripplemapped without reflections (requires particle effects) 5: ripples+reflections

name: r_slimestyle
description: See r_waterstyle, but affects only slime. If empty, defers to r_waterstyle.

name: r_lavastyle
description: See r_waterstyle, but affects only lava. If empty, defers to r_waterstyle.

name: r_telestyle
description: See r_waterstyle, but affects only teleporters. If empty, defers to r_waterstyle.

name: r_vertexdlights
description: Determine model lighting with respect to nearby dlights. Poor-man's rtlights.

name: vid_preservegamma
description: Restore initial hardware gamma ramps when quitting.

name: vid_hardwaregamma
description: Use hardware gamma ramps. 0=ugly texture-based gamma, 1=glsl(windowed) or hardware(fullscreen), 2=always glsl, 3=always hardware gamma (disabled if hardware doesn't support), 4=scene-only gamma.

name: vid_desktopgamma
description: Apply gamma ramps upon the desktop rather than the window.

name: r_fog_cullentities
description: 0: Never cull entities by fog... 1: Automatically cull entities according to fog. 2: Force fog culling regardless

name: r_fog_linear
description: 0: Use Exp/Exp2 fog. 1: Use linear fog.

name: r_fog_exp2
description: Expresses how fog fades with distance. 0 (matching DarkPlaces's default) is typically more realistic, while 1 (matching FitzQuake and others) is more common.

name: r_fog_permutation
description: Renders fog using a material permutation. 0 plays nicer with q3 shaders, but 1 is otherwise a better choice.

name: cl_csqc_nodeprecate
description: When set, disables deprecation warnings.

name: dpcompat_csqcinputeventtypes
description: Specifies the first csqc input event that the mod does not recognise. This should never have been a thing, but some mods are simply too buggy.

name: ignore_spec
description: 0: Never ignore spectators. 1: Ignore spectators only when playing. 2: Always ignore spectators even when spectating.

name: ignore_flood_duration
description: Time limit for inbound messages to be considered duplicates.

name: ignore_flood
description: Provides a way to reduce inbound spam from flooding out your chat (dupe messages are ignored). 0: No inbound flood protection. 1: Duplicate non-team messages will be filtered. 2: ALL duplicate messages will be filtered

name: ignore_opponents
description: 0: Don't ignore chat from enemies. 1: Always ignore chat from opponents (note: can also ignore f_ruleset checks). 2: Ignore chat from opponents only during a match (requires servers that actually reports match state).

name: cl_expsprite
description: Display a central sprite in explosion effects. QuakeWorld typically does so, NQ mods should not (which is problematic when played with the qw protocol).

name: r_explosionlight_colour
description: This controls the initial RGB values of EF_EXPLOSION effects.

name: r_explosionlight_fade
description: This controls the per-second RGB decay values of EF_EXPLOSION effects.

name: r_dimlight_colour
description: The red, green, blue, radius values for EF_DIMLIGHT effects (used for quad+pent in vanilla quake).

name: r_brightlight_colour
description: The red, green, blue, radius values for EF_BRIGHTLIGHT effects (unused in vanilla quake).

name: r_redlight_colour
description: The red, green, blue, radius values for EF_RED effects (typically used for pentagram in quakeworld).

name: r_greenlight_colour
description: The red, green, blue, radius values for EF_GREEN effects (rarely used).

name: r_bluelight_colour
description: The red, green, blue, radius values for EF_BLUE effects (typically used for quad-damage in quakeworld)

name: r_rocketlight_colour
description: This controls the RGB+radius values of MF_ROCKET effects.

name: r_muzzleflash_colour
description: This controls the initial RGB+radius of EF_MUZZLEFLASH/svc_muzzleflash effects.

name: r_muzzleflash_fade
description: This controls the per-second RGB+radius decay of EF_MUZZLEFLASH/svc_muzzleflash effects.

name: cl_truelightning
description: Manipulate the end position of the player's own beams, to hide lag. Can be set to fractional values to reduce the effect.

name: cl_beam_trace
description: Clips the length of any beams according to any walls they may have impacted.

name: cl_beam_alpha
description: Specifies the translucency of the lightning beam segments.

name: cl_legacystains
description: WARNING: this cvar will default to 0 and later removed at some point

name: cl_part_density_fade_start
description: Specifies the distance at which ssqc's pointparticles will start to get less dense.

name: cl_part_density_fade
description: Specifies the distance over which ssqc pointparticles density fades from all to none. If this is set to 0 then particles will spawn at their normal density regardless of location on the map.

name: capturerate
description: The framerate of the video to capture

name: capturedemowidth
description: When using capturedemo, this specifies the width of the FBO image used. Can be larger than your physical monitor.

name: capturedemoheight
description: When using capturedemo, this specifies the height of the FBO image used.

name: capturedriver
description: The driver to use to capture the demo. Use the capture command with no arguments for a list of drivers.

name: capturecodec
description: the compression/encoding codec to use.

name: capturesound
description: Enables the capturing of game voice. If not using capturedemo, this can be combined with cl_voip_test to capture your own voice.

name: capturethrottlesize
description: If set, capturing will slow down significantly if there is less disk space available than this cvar (in mb). This is useful when recording a stream to (ram)disk while another program is simultaneously consuming frames.

name: tts_mode
description: Text to speech 0: off 1: Read only chat messages with a leading 'tts ' prefix. 2: Read all chat messages 3: Read every single console print.

name: cl_chatmode
description: 0(nq) - everything is assumed to be a console command. prefix with 'say', or just use a messagemode bind 1(q3) - everything is assumed to be chat, unless its prefixed with a / 2(qw) - anything explicitly recognised as a command will be used as a command, anything unrecognised will be a chat message. / prefix is supported in all cases. ctrl held when pressing enter always makes any implicit chat into team chat instead.

name: con_textsize
description: Resize the console text to be a different height, scaled separately from the hud. The value is the height in (virtual) pixels.

name: con_savehistory
description: Write/update conhistory.txt

name: baseskin
description: The name of the player skin to use as a fallback.

name: con_stayhidden
description: 0: allow console to pounce on the user 1: console stays hidden unless explicitly invoked 2:toggleconsole command no longer works 3: shift+escape key no longer works

name: show_fps
