description: Displays the current framerate on-screen. 0: Off. 1: framerate average over a second. 2: Show a frametimes graph (with additional timing info). -1: Normalized graph that focuses on the variation ignoring base times.

name: scr_loadingrefresh
description: Force redrawing of the loading screen, in order to display EVERY resource that is loaded

name: scr_loadingscreen_aspect
description: Controls the aspect of levelshot images. 0: Use source image's aspect. 1: Force 4:3 aspect (ignore image's aspect), for best q3 compat. 2: Ignore aspect considerations and just smear it over the entire screen. -1: Disable levelshot use.

name: in_dinput
description: Enables the use of dinput for mouse movements

name: in_xinput
description: Enables the use of xinput for controllers. Note that if you have a headset plugged in, that headset will be used for audio playback if no specific audio device is configured.

name: in_nonstandarddeadkeys
description: Discard input events that result in multiple keys. Only the last key will be used. This results in behaviour that differs from eg notepad. To use a dead key, press it twice instead of the dead key followed by space.

name: in_rawinput
description: Enables rawinput support for mice in XP onwards. Rawinput permits independant device identification (ie: splitscreen clients can each have their own mouse)

name: in_rawinput_keyboard
description: Enables rawinput support for keyboards in XP onwards as well as just mice.

name: in_rawinput_rdp
description: Activate Remote Desktop Protocol devices too.

name: r_dodgytgafiles
description: Many old glquake engines had a buggy tga loader that ignored bottom-up flags. Naturally people worked around this and the world was plagued with buggy images. Most engines have now fixed the bug, but you can reenable it if you have bugged tga files.

name: r_dodgypcxfiles
description: When enabled, this will ignore the palette stored within pcx files, for compatibility with quake2.

name: r_dodgymiptex
description: When enabled, this will force regeneration of mipmaps, discarding mips1-4 like glquake did. This may eg solve fullbright issues with some maps, but may reduce distant detail levels.

name: r_keepimages
description: Retain unused images in memory for slightly faster map loading. 0: Redundant images will be purged after each map change. 1: Images will be retained until vid_reload (potentially consuming a lot of ram).

name: r_ignoremapprefixes
description: Ignores when textures were loaded from map-specific paths. 0: textures/foo/tex.tga will not be confused with textures/foo/tex.tga. 1: The same texture might be loaded multiple times over.

name: r_image_downloadsizelimit
description: The maximum allowed file size of images loaded from a web-based url. 0 disables completely, while empty imposes no limit.

name: scr_scoreboard_drawtitle
description: Wastes screen space when looking at the scoreboard.

name: scr_scoreboard_forcecolors
description: Makes the scoreboard colours obey enemycolor/teamcolor rules.

name: scr_scoreboard_newstyle
description: Display team colours and stuff in a style popularised by Electro. Looks more modern, but might not quite fit classic huds.

name: scr_scoreboard_showfrags
description: Display kills+deaths+teamkills, as determined by fragfile.dat-based conprint parsing. These may be inaccurate if you join mid-game.

name: scr_scoreboard_showlocation
description: Display player location names when playing mvd/qtv streams, if available.

name: scr_scoreboard_showhealth
description: Display health information when playing mvd/qtv streams. 0: off 1: on 2: show armour too. 3: combined health ('+' says more armour than health allows).

name: scr_scoreboard_showweapon
description: Display weapon information when playing mvd/qtv streams.

name: scr_scoreboard_showflags
description: Display flag caps+touches on the scoreboard, where our fragfile.dat supports them. 0: off 1: on 2: on only if someone appears to have interacted with a flag.

name: scr_scoreboard_fillalpha
description: Transparency amount for newstyle scoreboard.

name: scr_scoreboard_backgroundalpha
description: Further multiplier for the background alphas.

name: scr_scoreboard_teamscores
description: Makes +showscores act as +showteamscores. Because reasons.

name: scr_scoreboard_teamsort
description: On the scoreboard, sort players by their team BEFORE their personal score.

name: scr_scoreboard_afk
description: Show 'afk' in the packetloss column when they're afk.

name: scr_scoreboard_ping_status
description: Threshholds required to switch ping display from green to white, yellow, megenta and red.

name: sbar_teamstatus
description: Display the last team say from each of your team members just above the sbar area.

name: cl_sbaralpha
description: Specifies the transparency of the status bar. Only Takes effect when cl_sbar is set to 2.

name: sys_vibrate
description: Enables the system vibrator for damage events and such things. The value provided is a duration scaler.

name: sys_keepscreenon
description: If set, the screen will never darken. This might cost some extra battery power, but then so will running a 3d engine.

name: sys_orientation
description: Specifies what angle to render quake at. Valid values are: sensor (autodetect), landscape, portrait, reverselandscape, reverseportrait

name: r_projection
description: 0: regular perspective. 1: stereographic. 2: fisheye. 3: panoramic. 4: lambert azimuthal equal-area. 5: equirectangular. 6: panini.

name: ffov
description: Allows you to set a specific field of view for when a custom projection is specified. If empty, will use regular fov cvar, which might get messy.

name: itburnsitburnsmakeitstop
description: Ouch

name: cl_rollspeed
description: Controls the speed required to reach cl_rollangle's tilt.

name: cl_rollangle
description: Controls the maximum view should tilt while strafing.

name: cl_rollalpha
description: Controls the speed at which the view rolls according to sideways movement.

name: v_deathtilt
description: Specifies whether to tilt the view when dead.

name: cl_bob
description: Controls how much the camera position should bob up down as the player runs around.

name: cl_bobmodel
description: Controls whether the viewmodel should bob up and down as the player runs around.

name: v_kicktime
description: This controls how long viewangle changes from taking damage will last.

name: v_kickroll
description: This controls part of the strength of viewangle changes from taking damage.

name: v_kickpitch
description: This controls part of the strength of viewangle changes from taking damage.

name: v_idlescale
description: Enable swishing of the view (whether idle or otherwise). Often used for concussion effects.

name: crosshaircorrect
description: Moves the crosshair around to represent the impact point of a weapon positioned below the actual view position.

name: crosshairimage
description: Enables the use of an external/custom crosshair image

name: gl_polyblend
description: Controls whether temporary whole-screen colour changes should be honoured or not. Change gl_cshiftpercent if you want to adjust the intensity. This does not affect v_cshift commands sent from the server.

name: gl_polyblend_edgesize
description: This constrains colour shifts to the edge of the screen, with the value specifying the size of those borders.

name: v_bonusflash
description: Controls the strength of temporary screen flashes when picking up items (gl_polyblend must be enabled).

name: v_contentblend
description: Controls the strength of underwater colour tints (gl_polyblend must be enabled).

name: v_damagecshift
description: Controls the strength of damage-taken colour tints (gl_polyblend must be enabled).

name: v_quadcshift
description: Controls the strength of quad-damage colour tints (gl_polyblend must be enabled).

name: v_suitcshift
description: Controls the strength of envirosuit colour tints (gl_polyblend must be enabled).

name: v_ringcshift
description: Controls the strength of ring-of-invisibility colour tints (gl_polyblend must be enabled).

name: v_pentcshift
description: Controls the strength of pentagram-of-protection colour tints (gl_polyblend must be enabled).

name: v_gunkick
description: Controls the strength of view angle changes when firing weapons.

name: v_gunkick_q2
description: Controls the strength of view angle changes when firing weapons (in Quake2).

name: r_viewmodel_quake
description: Controls whether to use weird viewmodel movements from vanilla quake.

name: v_depthsortentities
description: Reorder entities for transparency such that the furthest entities are drawn first, allowing nearer transparent entities to draw over the top of them.

name: scr_autoid
description: Display nametags above all players while spectating.

name: scr_autoid_team
description: Display nametags above team members. 0: off. 1: display with half-alpha if occluded. 2: hide when occluded.

name: scr_autoid_health
description: Display health as part of nametags (when known).

name: scr_autoid_armor
description: Display armour as part of nametags (when known).

name: scr_autoid_weapon
description: Display the player's best weapon as part of their nametag (when known).

name: scr_autoid_weapon_mask
description: Mask of bits for weapon icons to actually show. +1: Shotgun. +2: Super Shotgun. +4: Nail Gun. +8: Super Nail Gun. +16: Grenade Launcher. +32: Rocket Launcher. +64: Lightning Showing only RL and GL is 96

name: scr_autoid_teamcolour
description: The colour for the text on the nametags of team members.

name: scr_autoid_enemycolour
description: The colour for the text on the nametags of non-team members.

name: v_cshift_empty
description: The colour tint to use when in the open air (additionally scaled by v_contentblend.

name: v_cshift_water
description: The colour tint to use when underwater (additionally scaled by v_contentblend.

name: v_cshift_slime
description: The colour tint to use when submerged in slime (additionally scaled by v_contentblend.

name: v_cshift_lava
description: The colour tint to use when burried in lava (ouchie!) (additionally scaled by v_contentblend.

name: m_forcewheel
description: 0: ignore mousewheels in apis where it is abiguous. 1: Use mousewheel when it is treated as a third axis. Motion above a threshold is ignored, to avoid issues with an unknown threshold. 2: Like 1, but excess motion is retained. The threshold specifies exact z-axis distance per notice.

name: m_forcewheel_threshold
description: Mousewheel graduations smaller than this will not trigger mousewheel deltas.

name: m_touchstrafe
description: 0: entire screen changes angles only. 1: right hand side controls strafing. 2:left hand side strafes.

name: m_fatpressthreshold
description: How fat your thumb has to be to register a fat press (touchscreens).

name: m_longpressthreshold
description: How long to press for it to register as a long press (touchscreens).

name: m_touchmajoraxis
description: When using a touchscreen, use only the major axis for strafing.

name: m_slidethreshold
description: How far your finger needs to move to be considered a slide event (touchscreens).

name: m_accel
description: Values >0 will amplify mouse movement proportional to velocity. Small values have great effect. A lot of good Quake Live players use around the 0.1-0.2 mark, but this depends on your mouse CPI and polling rate.

name: m_accel_style
description: 1 = Quake Live mouse acceleration, 0 = Old style accelertion.

name: m_accel_power
description: Used when m_accel_style is 1. Values 1 or below are dumb. 2 is linear and the default. 99% of accel users use this. Above 2 begins to amplify exponentially and you will get more acceleration at higher velocities. Great if you want low accel for slow movements, and high accel for fast movements. Good in combination with a sensitivity cap.

name: m_accel_offset
description: Used when m_accel_style is 1. Acceleration will not be active until the mouse movement exceeds this speed (counts per millisecond). Negative values are supported, which has the effect of causing higher rates of acceleration to happen at lower velocities.

name: m_accel_senscap
description: Used when m_accel_style is 1. Sets an upper limit on the amplified mouse movement. Great for tuning acceleration around lower velocities while still remaining in control of fast motion such as flicking.

name: joyadvaxisx_scale
description: ADVAXISSCALEDESC

name: joyadvaxisy_scale
description: ADVAXISSCALEDESC

name: joyadvaxisz_scale
description: ADVAXISSCALEDESC

name: joyadvaxisr_scale
description: ADVAXISSCALEDESC

name: joyadvaxisu_scale
description: ADVAXISSCALEDESC

name: joyadvaxisv_scale
description: ADVAXISSCALEDESC

name: joypitchsensitivity
description: ANGLESENSDESC

name: joyyawsensitivity
description: ANGLESENSDESC

name: joyrollsensitivity
description: ANGLESENSDESC

name: joypitchthreshold
description: ANGLETHRESHOLDDESC

name: joyyawthreshold
description: ANGLETHRESHOLDDESC

name: joyrollthreshold
description: ANGLETHRESHOLDDESC

name: joyexponent
description: Scales joystick/controller sensitivity non-linearly to increase precision in the center. A value of 1 is linear.

name: in_skipplayerone
description: Do not auto-assign joysticks/game-controllers to the first player. Requires in_restart to take effect.

name: cl_autotrack_team
description: Specifies a team name that should be auto-tracked (players on other teams will not be candidates for autotracking). Accepts * and ? wildcards for awkward chars.

name: cl_hightrack
description: Obsolete. If you want hightrack, use '[cl_]autotrack high' instead.

name: edit_stripcr
description: remove \r from eols (on load)

name: edit_addcr
description: make sure that each line ends with a \r (on save). Empty will be assumed to be 1 on windows and 0 otherwise.

name: edit_tabsize
description: How wide tab alignment is

name: pr_debugger
description: When enabled, QC errors and debug events will enable step-by-step tracing.

name: s_al_disable
description: 0: OpenAL works (generally as the highest priority). 1: OpenAL will be used only when a specific device is selected. 2: Don't allow ANY use of OpenAl. With OpenAL disabled, audio ouput will fall back to platform-specific output, avoiding miscilaneous third-party openal limitation bugs.

name: s_al_debug
description: Enables periodic checks for OpenAL errors.

name: s_al_hrtf
description: Enables use of HRTF, and which HRTF table to use. empty: auto, depending on openal config to enable it. \0: force off. 1: Use the default HRTF.

name: s_al_use_reverb
description: Controls whether reverb effects will be used. Set to 0 to block them. Reverb requires gamecode to configure the reverb properties, other than underwater.

name: s_al_reference_distance
description: This is the distance at which the sound is audiable with standard volume in the inverse distance models. Nearer sounds will be louder than the original sample.

name: s_al_velocityscale
description: Rescales velocity values, before doppler can be calculated.

name: wasapi_forcerate
description: Attempts to force snd_khz instead of using the system's default channel count. For this to work, you will need to set wasapi_exclusive 1

name: wasapi_forcechannels
description: Attempts to force snd_numchannels instead of using the system's default channel count. For this to work, you will need to set wasapi_exclusive 1

name: wasapi_exclusive
description: When set, attempts to take exclusive control of the output device, to the detriment of other programs (causing errors or even crashes in them). Exclusive mode leaves the game free to change the hardware's playback details, instead of being required to use a single system-wide 'mixer' rate. It should also reduce latency a little.

name: enemyforceskins
description: 0: Read the skin as-is. 1: Use the player's name instead of their skin setting. 2: Use their userid (probably too unreliable).3: Use a per-team player index.

name: r_tracker_frags
description: 0: like vanilla quake 1: shows only your kills/deaths 2: shows all kills

name: r_temporalscenecache
description: Controls whether to generate+reuse a scene cache over multiple frames. This is generated on a separate thread to avoid any associated costs. This can significantly boost framerates on complex maps, but can also stress the gpu more (performance tradeoff that varies per map). An outdated cache may be used if the cache takes too long to build (eg: lightmap animations), which could cause the odd glitch when moving fast (but retain more consistent framerates - another tradeoff). 0: Tranditional quake rendering. 1: Generate+Use the scene cache.

name: r_clutter_density
description: Scaler for clutter counts. 0 disables clutter completely. Clutter requires shaders with 'fte_clutter MODEL SPACING SCALEMIN SCALEMAX ZOFS ANGLEMIN ANGLEMAX' terms

name: r_clutter_distance
description: Distance at which clutter will become invisible.

name: r_decal_noperpendicular
description: When enabled, decals will not be generated on planes at a steep angle from clipped decal orientation.

name: r_particle_tracelimit
description: Number of traces to allow per frame for particle physics.

name: r_part_contentswitch
description: Enable particle effects to change based on content (ex. water).

name: r_part_classic_expgrav
description: Scaler for how fast classic explosion particles should accelerate due to gravity. 1 for like vanilla, 10 for like zquake.

name: r_part_classic_opaque
description: Disables transparency on classic particles, for the oldskool look.

name: r_part_classic_square
description: Enables square particles, for the oldskool look.

name: cl_crypt_rcon
description: Controls whether to send a hash instead of sending your rcon password as plain-text. Set to 1 for security, or 0 for backwards compatibility. Your command and any responses will still be sent as plain text. Instead, it is recommended to use rcon ONLY via dtls/tls/wss connections.

name: cl_shownet
description: Debugging var. 0 shows nothing. 1 shows incoming packet sizes. 2 shows individual messages. 3 shows entities too.

name: _cl_disconnectreason
description: This cvar contains the reason for the last disconnection, so that mod menus can know why things failed.

name: cl_pure
description: 0=standard quake rules. 1=clients should prefer files within packages present on the server. 2=clients should use *only* files within packages present on the server. Due to quake 1.01/1.06 differences, a setting of 2 is only reliable with total conversions. If sv_pure is set, the client will prefer the highest value set.

name: cl_maxfps
description: Sets the maximum allowed framerate. If you're using vsync or want to uncap framerates entirely then you should probably set this to 0. Set cl_yieldcpu 0 if you're trying to benchmark.

name: cl_maxfps_slop
description: If a frame is delayed (eg because of poor system timer precision), this is how much sooner to pretend the frame happened (in milliseconds). If it is set too low then the average framerate will drop below the target, while too high may result in excessively fast frames.

name: cl_idlefps
description: This is the maximum framerate to attain while idle/paused/unfocused.

name: cl_yieldcpu
description: Attempt to yield between frames. This can resolve issues with certain drivers and background software, but can mean less consistant frame times. Will reduce power consumption/heat generation so should be set on laptops or similar (over-hot/battery powered) devices.

name: cl_nolerp
description: Disables interpolation. If set, missiles/monsters will be show exactly what was last received, which will be jerky. Does not affect players. A value of 2 means 'interpolate only in single-player/coop'.

name: cl_nolerp_netquake
description: Disables interpolation when connected to an NQ server. Does affect players, even the local player. You probably don't want to set this.

name: cl_fullpitch
description: When set, attempts to unlimit the default view pitch. Note that some servers will screw over your angles if you use this, resulting in terrible gameplay, while some may merely clamp your angle serverside. This is also considered a cheat in quakeworld, ^1so this will not function there^7. For the equivelent in quakeworld, use serverinfo minpitch+maxpitch instead, which applies to all players fairly.

name: cl_vrui_force
description: Force the use of VR UIs, even with no VR headset active.

name: cl_vrui_lock
description: Controls how the UI is positioned when using VR/XR. 0: Repositioned infront of the head when the console/menus are toggled. 1: Locked infront of the reference position (which may require the user to turn around to find it).

name: cl_defaultport
description: The default port used to connect to servers.

name: cfg_save_name
description: This is the config name that is saved by default when no argument is specified.

name: cl_splitscreen
description: Enables splitscreen support. See also: allow_splitscreen, in_rawinput*, the "p" command.

name: lookspring
description: Recentre the camera when the mouse-look is released.

name: lookstrafe
description: Mouselook enables mouse strafing.

name: cl_lerp_maxinterval
description: Maximum interval between keyframes, in seconds. Larger values can result in entities drifting very slowly when they move sporadically.

name: cl_lerp_maxdistance
description: Maximum distance that an entity may move between snapshots without being considered as having teleported.

name: cl_lerp_players
description: Set this to make other players smoother, though it may increase effective latency. Affects only QuakeWorld.

name: cl_predict_players
description: Clear this cvar to see ents exactly how they are on the server.

name: cl_predict_players_frac
description: How much of other players to predict. Values less than 1 will help minimize overruns.

name: cl_predict_players_latency
description: Push the player back according to your latency, to give a smooth consistent simulation of the server.

name: cl_predict_players_nudge
description: An extra nudge of time, to cover video latency.

name: cl_solid_players
description: Consider other players as solid for player prediction.

name: cl_noblink
description: Disable the ^^b text blinking feature.

name: cl_servername
description: The hostname of the last server you connected to

name: cl_serveraddress
description: The address of the last server you connected to

name: record_flush
description: If set, explicitly flushes demo data to disk while recording. This may be inefficient, depending on how your operating system is configured.

name: cl_demoreel
description: When enabled, the engine will begin playing a demo loop on startup.

name: cl_loopbackprotocol
description: Which protocol to use for single-player/the internal client. Should be one of: qw, qwid, nqid, nq, fitz, bjp3, dp6, dp7, auto. If 'auto', will use qw protocols for qw mods, and nq protocols for nq mods.

name: cl_verify_urischeme
description: so its only really default.cfg that sets it*/

name: cl_fakeframes
description: Slow GPU? Want to see higher framerates get reported! Unleash the power of the lie to see much higher framerates! Many people said it couldn't be done, that the people wouldn't accept it, but to hell with the neighsayers and non-believers! WE WANT BIGGER NUMBERS AND WE'RE DAMN WELL GONNA GET THEM!... For best results, combine with an external tool like fluid motion frames...

name: cl_threadedphysics
description: When set, client input frames are generated and sent on a worker thread

name: r_drawflame
description: Set to -1 to disable ALL static entities. Set to 0 to disable only wall torches and standing flame. Set to 1 for everything drawn as normal.

name: rate
description: A rough measure of the bandwidth to try to use while playing. Too high a value may result in 'buffer bloat'.

name: drate
description: A rough measure of the bandwidth to try to use while downloading (in bytes per second).

name: msg
description: Filter console prints/messages. Only functions on QuakeWorld servers. 0=pickup messages. 1=death messages. 2=critical messages. 3=chat.

name: hand
description: For gamecode to know which hand to fire from. 0: Right 1: Left 2: Chest

name: cl_nofake
description: value 0: permits \r chars in chat messages value 1: blocks all \r chars value 2: allows \r chars, but only from teammates

name: r_torch
description: Generate a dynamic light at the player's position.

name: cl_r2g
description: Uses progs/grenade.mdl instead of progs/missile.mdl when 1.

name: cl_sendguid
description: Send a randomly generated 'globally unique' id to servers, which can be used by servers for score rankings and stuff. Different servers will see different guids. Delete the 'qkey' file in order to appear as a different user. If set to 2, all servers will see the same guid. Be warned that this can show other people the guid that you're using.

name: cl_downloads
description: Allows you to block all automatic downloads.

name: cl_download_csprogs
description: Download updated client gamecode if available. Warning: If you clear this to avoid downloading vm code, you should also clear cl_download_packages.

name: cl_download_redirection
description: Follow download redirection to download packages instead of individual files. Also allows the server to send nearly arbitary download commands. 2: allows redirection only to named packages files (and demos/*.mvd), which is a bit safer.

name: cl_download_packages
description: 0=Do not download packages simply because the server is using them. 1=Download and load packages as needed (does not affect games which do not use this package). 2=Do download and install permanently (use with caution!)

name: cl_download_wait
description: 0=join the game before downloads have even finished (might be laggy). 1=wait for all downloads to complete before joining.

name: mod_precache
description: Controls when models are loaded. 0: Load them only when they're actually needed. 1: Load them upfront. 2: Lazily load them to shorten load times at the risk of brief stuttering during only the start of the map.

name: gl_simpleitems
description: Replace models with simpler sprites.

name: cl_model_bobbing
description: Makes rotating pickup items bob too.

name: cl_countpendingpl
description: If set to 1, packet loss percentages will show packets still in transit as lost, even if they might still be received.

name: cl_standardchat
description: Disables auto colour coding in chat messages.

name: msg_filter
description: Filter out chat messages: 0=neither. 1=broadcast chat. 2=team chat. 3=all chat.

name: msg_filter_frags
description: Prevents frag messages from appearing on the console.

name: msg_filter_pickups
description: Prevents pickup messages from appearing on the console. This would normally be filtered by 'msg 1', but nq servers cannot respect that (nor nq mods running in qw servers).

name: cl_standardmsg
description: Disables auto colour coding in console prints.

name: cl_parsewhitetext
description: When parsing chat messages, enable support for messages like: red{white}red

name: cl_dlemptyterminate
description: Terminate downloads when reciving an empty download packet. This should help work around buggy mvdsv servers.

name: ruleset_allow_playercount
description: Specifies whether teamplay triggers that count nearby players are allowed in the current ruleset.

name: ruleset_allow_frj
description: Specifies whether Forward-Rocket-Jump scripts are allowed in the current ruleset. If 0, limits on yaw speed will be imposed so they cannot be scripted.

name: ruleset_allow_semicheats
description: If 0, this blocks a range of cvars that are marked as semi-cheats. Such cvars will be locked to their empty/0 value.

name: ruleset_allow_packet
description: If 0, network packets sent via the 'packet' command will be blocked. This makes scripting timers a little harder.

name: ruleset_allow_particle_lightning
description: A setting of 0 blocks using the particle system to replace lightning gun trails. This prevents making the trails thinner thus preventing them from obscuring your view of your enemies.

name: ruleset_allow_overlong_sounds
description: A setting of 0 will block the use of extra-long pickup sounds as item respawn timers.

name: ruleset_allow_larger_models
description: Enforces a maximum bounds limit on models, to prevent the use of additional spikes attached to the model from being used as a kind of wallhack.

name: ruleset_allow_modified_eyes
description: When 0, completely hides progs/eyes.mdl if it is not strictly identical to vanilla quake.

name: ruleset_allow_sensitive_texture_replacements
description: Allows the replacement of certain model textures (as well as the models themselves). This prevents adding extra fullbrights to make them blatently obvious.

name: ruleset_allow_localvolume
description: Allows the use of the snd_playersoundvolume cvar. Muting your own sounds can make it easier to hear where your opponent is.

name: ruleset_allow_shaders
description: When 0, this completely disables the use of external shader files, preventing custom shaders from being used for wallhacks.

name: ruleset_allow_fbmodels
description: When 1, allows all models to be displayed fullbright, completely ignoring the lightmaps. This feature exists only for parity with ezquake's defaults.

name: ruleset_allow_triggers
description: When 0, blocks the use of msg_trigger checks.

name: s_nominaldistance
description: This cvar defines how far an attenuation=1 sound can be heard.

name: mastervolume
description: Additional multiplier for all other sounds.

name: musicvolume
description: Volume level for background music.

name: volume
description: Volume level for game sounds (does not affect music, voice, or cinematics).

name: nosound
description: Disable all sound from the engine. Cannot be overriden by configs or anything if set via the -nosound commandline argument.

name: s_loadas8bit
description: Downsample sounds on load as lower quality 8-bit sound, to save memory.

name: snd_loadasstereo
description: Force mono sounds to load as if stereo ones, to waste memory. Not normally useful.

name: s_ambientlevel
description: This controls the volume levels of automatic area-based sounds (like water or sky), and is quite annoying. If you're playing deathmatch you'll definitely want this OFF.

name: s_khz
description: Sound speed, in kilohertz. Common values are 11, 22, 44, 48. Values above 1000 are explicitly in hertz.

name: s_inactive
description: Play sound while application is inactive (ie: tabbed out). Needs a snd_restart if changed.

name: s_mixahead
description: Specifies how many seconds to prebuffer audio. Lower values give less latency, but might result in crackling. Different hardware/drivers have different tolerances, and this setting may be ignored completely where drivers are expected to provide their own tolerances.

name: s_numspeakers
description: Number of hardware audio channels to use.

name: s_localvolume
description: Sound level for sounds local or originating from the player such as firing and pain sounds.

name: s_doppler
description: Enables doppler, with a multiplier for the scale.

name: s_doppler_min
description: Slowest allowed doppler scale.

name: s_doppler_max
description: Highest allowed doppler scale, to avoid things getting too weird.

name: snd_playbackrate
description: Debugging cvar that changes the playback rate of all new sounds.

name: snd_ignoregamespeed
description: When set, allows sounds to desynchronise with game time or demo speeds.

name: snd_ignorecueloops
description: Ignores cue commands in wav files, for q3 compat.

name: s_mixerthread
description: When enabled sound mixing will be run on a separate thread. Currently supported only by directsound. Other drivers may unconditionally thread audio. Set to 0 only if you have issues.

name: s_device
description: This is the sound device(s) to use, in the form of driver:device. If desired, multiple devices can be listed in space-seperated (quoted) tokens. _s_device_opts contains any enumerated options. In all seriousness, use the menu to set this if you wish to keep your hair.

name: _s_device_opts
description: The possible audio output devices, in "value" "description" pairs, for gamecode to read.

name: _cl_voip_capturedevice_opts
description: The possible audio capture devices, in "value" "description" pairs, for gamecode to read.

name: cl_voip_send
description: Sends voice-over-ip data to the server whenever it is set. 0: only send voice if +voip is pressed. 1: voice activation. 2: constantly send. +4: Do not send to game, only to rtp sessions.

name: cl_voip_test
description: If 1, enables you to hear your own voice directly, bypassing the server and thus without networking latency, but is fine for checking audio levels. Note that sv_voip_echo can be set if you want to include latency and packetloss considerations, but setting that cvar requires server admin access and is thus much harder to use.

name: cl_voip_vad_threshhold
description: This is the threshhold for voice-activation-detection when sending voip data

name: cl_voip_vad_delay
description: Keeps sending voice data for this many seconds after voice activation would normally stop

name: cl_voip_capturingvol
description: Volume multiplier applied while capturing, to avoid your audio from being heard by others. Does not affect game volume when others speak (minimum of cl_voip_capturingvol and cl_voip_ducking is used).

name: cl_voip_showmeter
description: Shows your speech volume above the standard hud. 0=hide, 1=show when transmitting, 2=ignore voice-activation disable

name: cl_voip_ducking
description: Scales game audio by this much when someone is talking to you. Does not affect your speaker volume when you speak (minimum of cl_voip_capturingvol and cl_voip_ducking is used).

name: cl_voip_micamp
description: Amplifies your microphone when using voip.

name: cl_voip_codec
description: 0: speex(@11khz). 1: raw. 2: opus. 3: speex(@8khz). 4: speex(@16). 5:speex(@32). 6: pcma. 7: pcmu.

name: cl_voip_noisefilter
description: Enable the use of the noise cancelation filter.

name: cl_voip_autogain
description: Attempts to normalize your voice levels to a standard level. Useful for lazy people, but interferes with voice activation levels.

name: cl_voip_bitrate
description: For codecs with non-specific bitrates, this specifies the target bitrate to use.

name: pkg_autoupdate
description: Controls autoupdates, can only be changed via the downloads menu. 0: off. 1: enabled (stable only). 2: enabled (unstable). Note that autoupdate will still prompt the user to actually apply the changes.

name: vk_stagingbuffers
description: Configures which dynamic buffers are copied into gpu memory for rendering, instead of reading from shared memory. Empty for default settings. Accepted chars are u(niform), e(lements), v(ertex), 0(none).

name: vk_submissionthread
description: Execute submits+presents on a thread dedicated to executing them. This may be a significant speedup on certain drivers.

name: vk_debug
description: Register a debug handler to display driver/layer messages. 2 enables the standard validation layers.

name: vk_dualqueue
description: Attempt to use a separate queue for presentation. Blank for default.

name: vk_busywait
description: Force busy waiting until the GPU finishes doing its thing.

name: vk_waitfence
description: Waits on fences, instead of semaphores. This is more likely to result in gpu stalls while the cpu waits.

name: vk_usememorypools
description: Allocates memory pools for sub allocations. Vulkan has a limit to the number of memory allocations allowed so this should always be enabled, however at this time FTE is unable to reclaim pool memory, and would require periodic vid_restarts to flush them.

name: vk_khr_get_memory_requirements2
description: Enable extended memory info querires

name: vk_khr_dedicated_allocation
description: Flag vulkan memory allocations as dedicated, where applicable.

name: vk_khr_push_descriptor
description: Enables better descriptor streaming.

name: vk_amd_rasterization_order
description: Enables the use of relaxed rasterization ordering, for a small speedup at the minor risk of a little zfighting.

name: vK_khr_fragment_shading_rate
description: Enables the use of variable shading rates.

name: vk_ext_astc_decode_mode
description: Enables reducing texture cache sizes for LDR ASTC-compressed textures.

name: vk_khr_ray_query
description: Required for the use of hardware raytraced shadows.

name: r_shadow_scissor
description: constrains stencil shadows to the onscreen box that contains the maxmium extents of the light. This avoids unnecessary work.

name: r_shadow_realtime_world
description: Enables the use of static/world realtime lights.

name: r_shadow_realtime_world_lightmaps
description: Specifies how much of the map's normal lightmap to retain when using world realtime lights. 0 completely replaces lighting.

name: r_shadow_realtime_world_importlightentitiesfrommap
description: Controls default loading of map-based realtime lights. 0: Load explicit .rtlight files only. 1: Load explicit lights then try fallback to parsing the entities lump. 2: Load only the entities lump.

name: r_shadow_realtime_dlight
description: Enables the use of dynamic realtime lights, allowing explosions to use bumpmaps etc properly.

name: r_shadow_realtime_dlight_shadows
description: Allows dynamic realtime lights to cast shadows as they move.

name: r_shadow_playershadows
description: Controls the presence of shadows on the local player.

name: r_shadow_raytrace
description: Enables use of hardware raytracing for shadows. Consider also using with r_halfrate.

name: r_shadow_shadowmapping
description: Enables soft shadows instead of stencil shadows.

name: r_shadow_shadowmapping_precision
description: Scales the shadowmap detail level up or down.

name: r_shadow_shadowmapping_depthbits
description: Shadowmap depth bits. 16, 24, or 32.

name: r_sun_dir
description: Specifies the direction that crepusular rays appear along

name: r_sun_colour
description: Specifies the colour of sunlight that appears in the form of crepuscular rays.

name: r_shadows_fakedistance
description: The radius to use for fake shadows.

name: r_shadows_throwdirection
description: The direction to throw the fake shadows in. Should ideally be opposite to r_sun_dir, but that just shows how fake these things actually are.

name: r_shadows_focus
description: Offset for the center of the fake-shadows volume.

name: mod_terrain_networked
description: Terrain edits are networked. Clients will download sections on demand, and servers will notify clients of changes.

name: mod_terrain_defaulttexture
description: Newly created terrain tiles will use this texture. This should generally be updated by the terrain editor.

name: mod_terrain_savever
description: Which terrain section version to write if terrain was edited.

name: mod_terrain_sundir
description: The direction of the sun (vector will be normalised).

name: mod_terrain_ambient
description: Proportion of ambient light.

name: mod_terrain_shadows
description: Cast rays to determine whether parts of the terrain should be in shadow.

name: mod_terrain_shadow_dist
description: How far rays should be cast in order to look for occlusing geometry.

name: mod_map_lights
description: Calculates lighting on brushes/patches.

name: mod_map_texscale
description: Defines the scale of texture texels. Use 1 for quake+quake2 maps, and 0.5 for quake3 maps.

name: gl_affinemodels
description: Use affine texture sampling for models. This replicates software rendering's distortions.

name: r_vertexlight
description: Hack loaded shaders to remove detail pass and lightmap sampling for faster rendering.

name: r_forceprogramify
description: Reduce the shader to a single texture, and then make stuff up about its mother. The resulting fist fight results in more colour when you shine a light upon its face. Set to 2 to ignore 'depthfunc equal' and 'tcmod scale' in order to tolerate bizzare shaders made for a bizzare engine. Because most shaders made for DP are by people who _clearly_ have no idea what the heck they're doing, you'll typically need the '2' setting.

name: dpcompat_nopremulpics
description: By default FTE uses premultiplied alpha for hud/2d images, while DP does not (which results in halos with low-res content). Unfortunately DDS files would need to be recompressed, resulting in visible issues.

name: r_glsl_precache
description: Force all relevant glsl permutations to load upfront.

name: r_halfrate
description: Use half-rate shading (where supported by gpu).

name: gl_skyboxdist
description: The distance of the skybox. If 0, the engine will determine it based upon the far clip plane distance.

name: r_skycloudalpha
description: Controls how opaque the front layer of legacy scrolling skies should be.

name: r_glsl_skybox_orientation
description: Defines the axis around which skyboxes will rotate (the first three values). The fourth value defines the speed the skybox rotates at, in degrees per second.

name: r_glsl_skybox_autorotate
description: Defines the axis around which skyboxes will rotate (the first three values). The fourth value defines the speed the skybox rotates at, in degrees per second.

name: r_skyfog
description: This controls an alpha-blend value for fog on skyboxes, cumulative with regular fog alpha.

name: gl_blacklist_texture_compression
description: When enabled, blocks recognition of all compressed-texture formats. Does NOT block support for gl3 texture types like e5bgr9 nor rgba16f.

name: gl_blacklist_generatemipmap
description: Self-generate mipmaps, instead of letting the graphics driver do it.

name: r_bloom
description: Enables bloom (light bleeding from bright objects). Fractional values reduce the amount shown.

name: r_bloom_retain
description: How much of the regular scene to retain when bloom is active.

name: r_bloom_filter
description: Controls how bright the image must get before it will bloom (3 separate values, in RGB order).

name: r_bloom_size
description: Target bloom kernel size (assuming a video width of 320).

name: r_bloom_downsize
description: Technically more correct with a value of 1, but you probably won't notice.

name: r_bloom_initialscale
description: Initial scaling factor for bloom. should be either 1 or 0.5

name: r_editlights_import_radius
description: changes size of light entities loaded from a map

name: r_editlights_import_ambient
description: ambient light scaler for imported lights

name: r_editlights_import_diffuse
description: diffuse light scaler for imported lights

name: r_editlights_import_specular
description: specular light scaler for imported lights

name: r_editlights
description: enables .rtlights file editing mode. Consider using csaddon/equivelent instead.

name: r_editlights_cursordistance
description: maximum distance of cursor from eye

name: r_editlights_cursorpushoff
description: how far to push the cursor off the impacted surface

name: r_editlights_cursorpushback
description: how far to pull the cursor back toward the eye, for some reason

name: r_editlights_cursorgrid
description: snaps cursor to this grid size

name: mod_external_vis
description: Attempt to load .vis patches for quake maps, allowing transparent water to work properly.

name: mod_warnmodels
description: Warn if any models failed to load. Set to 0 if your mod is likely to lack optional models (like its in development).

name: mod_litsprites_force
description: If set to 1, sprites will be lit according to world lighting (including rtlights), like Tenebrae. Ideally use EF_ADDITIVE or EF_FULLBRIGHT to make emissive sprites instead.

name: mod_loadmappackages
description: Load additional content embedded within bsp files.

name: mod_lightscale_broken
description: When active, replicates a bug from vanilla - the radius of r_dynamic lights is scaled by per-surface texture scale rather than using actual distance.

name: mod_lightpoint_distance
description: This is the maximum distance to trace when searching for a ground surface for lighting info on map formats without light more fancy lighting info. Use 2048 for full compat with Quake.

name: r_sprite_backfacing
description: Make oriented sprites face backwards relative to their orientation, for compat with q1.

name: r_nolerp_list
description: Models in this list will not interpolate. Any models included here should be considered bad.

name: r_noshadow_list
description: Models in this list will not cast shadows.
